roadmap.artifact: Execution roadmap for the 60 verified/deduped W921 specs + the 100x demo spec
roadmap.mode: READ-ONLY analysis of product code. Roadmap only. Files under audit/w921 are the only authorized writes.
roadmap.executive_summary: 60 verified specs span 8 product surfaces plus the demo rebuild. Two source files dominate the contention map and force a SEQUENTIAL LANE: src/router.js (touched by ~17 specs: all of Gateway, all of Govern, Autopilot bandit + sequential-AB, plus Website pricing/trust/hero/error-boundary) and cli/kolm.js (touched by ~33 specs: all of CLI, Studio, Run, Data Engine, plus Autopilot and several Govern). Everything that creates NEW isolated modules (semantic-router.js, provider-health.js, gateway-guardrail.js, conformal.js, drift-stats.js, etc.) or NEW worker/python files (workers/data, apps/trainer, apps/runtime) or NEW build scripts (scripts/build-*.cjs) or NEW client assets (public/account-*.js, public/*.css) can be authored 100% in parallel; only the final WIRE step that edits router.js or cli/kolm.js must serialize through the big-file lane. The demo rebuild is fully parallel-safe (it touches only public/demo-live*, data/demo, scripts/capture-demo-timeline.mjs, scripts/demo-claim-verify.cjs, scripts/release-verify.cjs, public/sw.js — none of the two big files). The highest-leverage, most-differentiating work concentrates in the Gateway (semantic routing + injection guardrail + circuit breaker + true streaming + semantic cache all stamped into the existing non-signed receipt block — turning resilience, safety and savings into AUDITABLE proof no competitor offers) and in Studio/Run (a real logit-level distillation objective + multi-LoRA serving + EAGLE speculative decoding, the engine kolm sells but has not fully wired). CRITICAL SEQUENCING NOTE: three pre-existing w920 P0 defects on the gateway hot path (output-redaction never reaching the client, fallback_reason enum drift that can THROW and abort a call, cost_usd silently defaulting to 0) must be fixed FIRST because four W921 gateway specs build on that path and one (semantic router) explicitly depends on the cost_usd fix and on a quality-label/prompt-text capture pipeline the lake does not yet store. Recommended order: Phase 0 fix the gateway P0 trust bugs + build the additive non-signed receipt-field convention once; Phase 1 author all NEW-module/worker/script/asset groups in parallel; Phase 2 run the two big-file wire lanes serially (router lane, then/alongside cli lane) integrating each completed module; Phase 3 demo rebuild + release-verify gates + sw.js bump.

big_file_lanes.router_lane: src/router.js — SEQUENTIAL. Every spec whose final wiring edits router.js must land one-at-a-time to avoid merge collisions in the dispatch handler (~6131-6584) and route table (~21396-23733). Order by dependency: P0 bugfixes -> guardrail -> circuit breaker -> semantic cache -> true streaming -> semantic router (needs cost_usd fix) -> OTel gen_ai -> Govern routes (anchor/c2pa/ai-act/drift/intoto) -> Website routes (status/pricing/error-sink/hero) -> Autopilot routes (bandit/sequential).
big_file_lanes.cli_lane: cli/kolm.js — SEQUENTIAL but INDEPENDENT of the router lane (different file), so it can run as a second concurrent lane. Order: CLI primitives first (kolm-ux.js prompt/task-tree, cred-store, extensions) since other verbs reuse them, then Data Engine verbs, Studio verbs, Run verbs, then the optional CLI surfacing for Autopilot/Govern/Gateway.

top_leverage.technique: Cost/latency/quality-aware semantic router (Avengers-Pro cluster/KNN over kolm's own captured outcomes) stamped into the non-signed receipt.router_decision block
top_leverage.why: kolm's central pitch — 'cheapest model that clears the bar, with a receipt to prove it' — is today UNREALIZED: routing is a static chain plus a caller-supplied confidence number kolm never computes. This is the single biggest gap-to-pitch on the flagship revenue route, and stamping route_score + rejected candidates into the existing additive non-signed receipt field makes the savings AUDITABLE — a differentiator RouteLLM/LiteLLM/OpenRouter cannot match. Caveat: the verdict flags a real prerequisite (the lake stores no quality/win-loss label and no prompt text), so v1 ships as a cost+latency reorderer and the quality term needs a capture-pipeline addition (adjusted 13d).

top_leverage.technique: Inline prompt-injection / jailbreak guardrail wired into the dispatch input stage, verdict stamped into the signed-receipt-adjacent non-signed block
top_leverage.why: OWASP LLM01:2025, the #1 LLM risk and table-stakes vs Cloudflare/Portkey. The classifier already exists, is tested, and never throws — it is merely UNWIRED from the live path. 3-day, zero-new-dependency win that blocks an attack BEFORE any upstream token is spent ($0 rejection) and lets kolm uniquely ATTEST the screening verdict in a verifiable receipt. Lowest-risk/highest-impact item in the whole wave (verdict: scopeCorrect+depsComplete, 3d).

top_leverage.technique: Health-aware adaptive failover: per-provider circuit breaker (Resilience4j 3-state + Envoy exponential ejection) + health-aware load balancing, with a fail-open panic invariant
top_leverage.why: Today a hard-down primary is re-fired on EVERY request at full timeout (60s default, up to 300s), cascading one provider outage into a tenant-wide latency cliff and inflating the very wrapper-tax the receipts measure. A breaker that skips known-dead providers (and stamps circuit_open as a verifiable route decision) converts resilience into the same audit-trail differentiator. Must enforce never-eject-the-last-provider so the breaker can only improve availability.

top_leverage.technique: Trainer-stack unification + a real logit-level KD objective (DistiLLM-2 contrastive SKL+SRKL / KL) promoted into the shipping path
top_leverage.why: kolm sells distillation, but the actual distillation OBJECTIVE (logit-level KD loss) is the critical w920 Studio gap (#0, 0.85) and the two trainer stacks diverge. Unifying them and shipping the real contrastive loss is what makes the Trinity story technically true rather than a SeqKD approximation. Two critical-impact specs converge here (unification + DistiLLM-2).

top_leverage.technique: Multi-LoRA serving (one base, N adapters, per-request switch on one GPU) wired into serve.py + deploy generators
top_leverage.why: Critical-impact (run.frontier #0, 0.9): the Python is implemented but NOT wired into the serve path, so the marquee 'distill many small adapters, serve them cheaply' economics are not deliverable. Unlocks the per-skill adapter business model and pairs directly with the Studio model-merge + LoRA-variant specs.

top_leverage.technique: Fix fallback_reason enum drift (latent P0 that can THROW and abort a gateway call) + adopt the additive non-signed / RFC-8785 receipt discipline, then Sigstore/Rekor per-call receipt anchoring
top_leverage.why: The enum-drift bug (govern.frontier #0, critical 0.9) is a live abort risk that the new gateway specs (which add 'circuit_open' to the enum) would otherwise trip. Once the receipt path is hardened, transparency-log anchoring of per-call receipts turns kolm's signed-receipt story into independently-verifiable provenance — the deepest moat in the product.

top_leverage.technique: Demo rebuild (re-root the cinematic engine in a captured REAL CLI run with an in-browser Ed25519 verify climax) + single-source-of-truth pricing coherence + the live hero demo
top_leverage.why: The current /demo-live is 100% fabricated string literals with a climax that points at a 404 — the #1 'canned demo' credibility-killer. Re-rooting it in a checked-in real run with a self-contained, zero-network, in-browser receipt verification makes the product's core claim impossible to fake. Pricing coherence is critical-impact (incoherent numbers across homepage/pricing/compare actively erode trust). These are parallel-safe and convert the proof story into the conversion story.

top_leverage.technique: Sequential always-valid A/B testing (GAVI + mSPRT) + replace the toy stump-GBM meta-model with a real gradient-boosted regressor + conformal-calibrated K-Score intervals
top_leverage.why: The autopilot deploy guardrail currently gates on a point estimate from a depth-1 stump and a fixed-horizon test — statistically indefensible for an autonomous re-distill/deploy loop. Anytime-valid stats (critical-impact) + a real GBM + conformal lower-bound gating make the self-improving loop trustworthy enough to actually run unattended, which is the entire autopilot value proposition.

specs.technique: Replace the toy depth-1 stump-GBM meta-model with a real gradient-boosted regressor (depth-3 trees + row/column subsampling + L2 shrinkage in pure JS as the always-available default; optional XGBoost/LightGBM-class Python worker shell at scale) WRAPPED in conformalized-quantile-regression (CQR / split-conformal) so the K-Score prediction carries a distribution-free, finite-sample-calibrated interval instead of a heuristic n/1000 confidence and an arbitrary fixed band.
specs.surface: Autopilot / Autonomous Agent — the meta-learner (kolm-meta-trainer) that predicts K-Score (and compile-time / failure-mode) from data-feature vectors, consumed by quality-predictor.predictKScore -> compile-simulator, cost-optimizer, autopilot-lifecycle deploy guardrail, and student-arch-recommender.
specs.how_it_works:
PIPELINE PLACEMENT. The meta-model sits at the bottom of the autopilot intelligence stack. Every economic decision the daemon makes — simulateCompile (compile vs skip on delta_k), cost-optimizer.rankStrategies (delta_K / dollar), _evaluateDeploy (promote only when the prediction clears the ship gate), student-arch-recommender (which backbone) — is gated on a single number: predicted K-Score, plus an interval. quality-predictor.predictKScore is the entry point; below MIN_ROWS_FOR_META (1000) it uses a hand-calibrated heuristic, at/above it flips to the learned GBM in src/kolm-meta-trainer.js (W832). That GBM is the toy this spec replaces.

WHY GBT IS THE RIGHT FAMILY (math + evidence). The meta-task is small-n tabular regression: a feature matrix X (n_pairs, dup_fraction, coverage_score, avg_quality, cot_contam_fraction, teacher_diversity, plus the W832 META_FEATURES) -> scalar target y = observed K in [0,1], with n in the hundreds-to-few-thousand range. The 2024 'Comprehensive Benchmark of ML and DL across diverse tabular datasets' (arXiv:2408.14817) shows tree ensembles win the MAJORITY of tabular tasks and KEEP winning even when large sets are down-sampled to 1000 rows (CatBoost best on 19/111 = 17.1% vs best DL 9.9%; on <1000-row sets H2O-GBM 16.6% vs best DL ResNet 13.9%). This is exactly kolm's regime, so a real GBM (not a NN, not a single tree) is the correct upgrade.

WHAT 'REAL GBM' MEANS vs THE CURRENT STUMP. Gradient boosting fits an additive model F_M(x) = sum_{m=1..M} eta * h_m(x), where each h_m is a regression tree fit to the negative gradient (for squared loss, the residual r_i = y_i - F_{m-1}(x_i)). The current code (_trainOneTargetGBM) restricts EVERY h_m to a depth-1 stump (one split, one feature) with NO regularization, NO row subsampling, NO column subsampling, and a constant 0 baseline. Depth-1 stumps cannot represent ANY feature interaction (e.g. 'more pairs only helps when dup_fraction is low'), which is the dominant structure in data-quality->K. The fix is the textbook GBT recipe (Friedman 2001 'Greedy Function Approximation'; Chen & Guestrin 2016 'XGBoost'): (1) depth-D trees (D=3 captures up to 3-way interactions); (2) the regularized split objective Gain = 1/2 * [ G_L^2/(H_L+lambda) + G_R^2/(H_R+lambda) - (G_L+G_R)^2/(H_L+H_R+lambda) ] - gamma, where for squared loss g_i = -(y_i - F(x_i)) and h_i = 1 (so leaf weight w = -G/(H+lambda) = mean residual shrunk by L2 lambda); (3) Friedman stochastic subsampling (row fraction subsample=0.8, feature fraction colsample=0.7) which both regularizes and decorrelates trees; (4) baseline F_0 = mean(y) instead of 0 so the first tree models deviation not level; (5) min_child_weight (min samples per leaf) and early stopping on a holdout to pick M. All of this is implementable in pure JS at the N<=50k scale the file already targets — it is the same brute-force best-split scan, just recursed to depth D over a column/row subsample with the regularized gain.

WHY UNCERTAINTY MUST BE CALIBRATED, NOT HEURISTIC. Today inferKolmMeta returns confidence = n_train_rows/1000 (a SUPPORT proxy, explicitly 'NOT a posterior') and quality-predictor turns it into a fixed band half = 0.155 - 0.11*confidence. An autonomous system that SPENDS GPU and DEPLOYS models on an uncalibrated band is fragile: when the true conditional variance is high (sparse region of feature space) the band can be far too tight, so _evaluateDeploy promotes on false confidence. Two production-grade ways to get real uncertainty: (A) NGBoost / CatBoost-RMSEWithUncertainty — natural-gradient boosting that fits the parameters (mu, sigma) of a conditional Normal by minimizing NLL, giving a native predictive distribution. BUT the parametric-Normal assumption is fragile: Manokhin's published evaluation shows NGBoost's nominal 95% interval covered only 91% and the 80% interval only 74%, and HPO flipped it from over- to under-confident — i.e. NOT reliably calibrated. (B) CONFORMALIZED QUANTILE REGRESSION (Romano, Patterson, Candes 2019, arXiv:1905.03222) / split-conformal — wrap ANY point regressor (our real GBM) and produce intervals with a DISTRIBUTION-FREE finite-sample marginal coverage guarantee P(y in C(x)) >= 1 - alpha under exchangeability, with NO distributional assumption. Concretely: fit two quantile GBMs q_lo (alpha/2) and q_hi (1-alpha/2) via pinball loss on a training split, then on a held-out calibration split compute conformity scores E_i = max(q_lo(x_i) - y_i, y_i - q_hi(x_i)), take Q = the ceil((1-alpha)(n_cal+1))/n_cal empirical quantile of {E_i}, and emit the calibrated interval [q_lo(x) - Q, q_hi(x) + Q]. This is the technique Manokhin and the MAPIE library (scikit-learn-contrib, arXiv:2207.12274) recommend over NGBoost precisely because coverage tracks the nominal level regardless of the K-distribution's shape. DECISION: ship the REAL GBM as the point predictor and CQR (or simple split-conformal absolute-residual when quantile heads aren't available) for the interval. This is one technique that satisfies BOTH halves of the candidate ('real GBM' + 'native/calibrated uncertainty') AND fully subsumes the discovery doc's separate 'conformal uncertainty for the K-Score' candidate, AND supplies the calibrated variance the bandit + sequential-A/B candidates consume — the highest-leverage cluster, shared off ONE feed.

DATA FLOW. (1) backfillFromRuns + appendTrainingRow already accumulate (features -> observed K) rows in ~/.kolm/meta-training/rows.jsonl, tenant-fenced, idempotent by run_id. (2) On retrain, split rows into train / calibration (e.g. 70/30, or use CV+ jackknife-plus from MAPIE when n is small to avoid wasting data). (3) Fit the real GBM mean head + (optionally) two pinball-loss quantile heads on the train split. (4) Compute the conformal quantile Q on the calibration split; persist {model, conformal_Q, alpha, n_cal} to ~/.kolm/meta-model.json under the SAME META_VERSION feature-order fence. (5) inferKolmMeta returns {kscore_predicted, ci:[lo,hi] (conformally calibrated), coverage_target:1-alpha, basis:'learned_gbm', n_train_rows}. (6) quality-predictor._predictLearned passes the conformal CI straight through (no more 0.155-0.11*conf band) and sets confidence from interval WIDTH (narrow => high). (7) WORKER-SCALE PATH: when n grows past a configurable ceiling or KOLM_META_BACKEND=xgb is set AND python3 + xgboost detected, kolm-meta-trainer delegates to a new workers/meta-train/meta-train.mjs Node shell that spawns apps/trainer/meta_xgb.py (XGBoost/LightGBM + MAPIE CQR), reads back the SAME envelope JSON. If python/xgboost is absent, it FALLS BACK to the pure-JS real GBM (honest, never claims xgboost it does not have) — identical contract to workers/quantize and workers/multimodal-redact-audio.
specs.gap: (1) DEPTH-1 STUMPS cannot model feature interactions — the dominant structure in data-quality->K (e.g. pairs help only when dup is low) — so the learned path is systematically biased exactly where the autopilot most needs accuracy. (2) NO regularization / subsampling / early stopping => the 50-stump ensemble can overfit small backfilled sets with no defense and no held-out stopping. (3) CONFIDENCE IS NOT CALIBRATED: n/1000 is a support proxy, and the downstream CI (0.155 - 0.11*conf) is a hand-picked band with NO coverage guarantee; an interval that nominally says 90% may cover far less in sparse feature regions, and the autonomous _evaluateDeploy path promotes on it. (4) The advertised production path (apps/trainer/meta_xgb.py worker shell) DOES NOT EXIST — the 'meta-distillation' headline is backed only by the toy. (5) The qp->meta feature projection (_toMetaFeatures) maps onto 'arbitrary-but-fixed proxy slots' so even the toy's learned accuracy is semantically unverified. Net: the single number every autopilot money/deploy decision rests on is produced by an explicitly-toy model with uncalibrated uncertainty.
specs.improvement: Two-tier, drop-in, contract-preserving replacement under the existing META_VERSION feature-order fence (bump to 'w921-v1'). TIER 1 (always available, zero new deps, the default): upgrade src/kolm-meta-trainer.js from depth-1 stumps to a REAL pure-JS gradient-boosted regressor — recursive depth-D (default 3) regression trees, regularized split gain with L2 lambda + gamma + min_child_weight, Friedman row subsample + per-tree column subsample, F_0 = mean(y) baseline, and early stopping on an internal holdout to choose n_trees. ACCURACY: real-GBM K-Score RMSE must beat the current depth-1 stump GBM by >=20% relative on the same rows (a depth-3 ensemble vs depth-1 stumps on interacting features typically improves RMSE 15-40%); and beat the cold-start heuristic K (CAL_SLOPE/CAL_INTERCEPT) by >=10% RMSE at n>=300. CALIBRATION (the headline): the conformal 90% interval must achieve empirical coverage in [0.86, 0.94] on held-out folds (vs the current band which has NO coverage guarantee and, per the NGBoost evidence, parametric bands routinely miss by 5-6 points). SHARPNESS: mean interval width should be <= the current fixed band's width (0.10-0.22) at matched coverage. DETERMINISM: identical rows + seed => byte-identical model (preserve the W832 determinism promise). LATENCY: pure-JS retrain at n=2000, depth-3, 200 trees < 2s on a laptop core (keeps the daemon tick cheap). XGBoost worker path: match scikit/MAPIE reference coverage within +/-2 points.

specs.how_it_works:
GOAL: replace quality-predictor.js's admittedly-fabricated CI (heuristic half = 0.26 - 0.32*confidence; learned half = 0.155 - 0.11*confidence) with a DISTRIBUTION-FREE, FINITE-SAMPLE prediction interval that ACTUALLY covers the true K-Score at a stated rate (e.g. 90%), plus a principled ABSTAIN signal when the interval straddles the ship gate (0.85). 

SPLIT (INDUCTIVE) CONFORMAL — REGRESSION, exact algorithm (Lei et al. 2018; Angelopoulos & Bates 2021): we already have a point predictor f_hat(x) = predictKScore over a feature vector x and a growing pool of (features -> observed K) labelled rows. (1) Hold out a CALIBRATION set of n rows DISJOINT from anything used to fit f_hat. (2) For each calibration row compute the nonconformity score s_i = |y_i - f_hat(x_i)| (absolute residual of observed K vs predicted K). (3) Sort the scores; take the conformal quantile q_hat = s_(b) where b = ceil((n+1)*(1-alpha)) (the b-th smallest score). (4) For a NEW vector x return the interval C(x) = [f_hat(x) - q_hat, f_hat(x) + q_hat], clamped to [0,1]. THEOREM (exchangeability): 1-alpha <= P(Y_new in C(x_new)) <= 1-alpha + 1/(n+1). This is a finite-sample marginal coverage guarantee with NO distributional assumption on K. 

SMALL-n GUARD: if b > n (equivalently n < 1/alpha - 1, e.g. n < 19 for alpha=0.10, n < 99 for alpha=0.01) the quantile is undefined and the only valid interval is [0,1] (infinite / unbounded). The module MUST detect this and either return the trivial [0,1] interval with basis:'conformal_undercalibrated' OR fall back to the existing heuristic band — never silently return a too-tight conformal interval. This is the SAME flip pattern quality-predictor already uses (heuristic below MIN_ROWS_FOR_META, learned above): below MIN_CONFORMAL_CAL (set to ceil(1/alpha)+1 = 21 at alpha=0.10, a safe floor) we keep the transparent heuristic band; at/above it we attach the conformal interval. 

LOCALLY-WEIGHTED (ADAPTIVE) VARIANT (Lei et al. 'split-localized'; SLCP): fixed split-conformal intervals are CONSTANT width for every x, which is wrong for heteroskedastic K (cold-start vectors are far noisier than Trinity-like ones). Normalize the score s_i = |y_i - f_hat(x_i)| / sigma_hat(x_i) where sigma_hat is a cheap spread estimate (we already have one: 1 - predictor confidence, or a kNN residual estimate over the calibration set). Then the interval is f_hat(x) +- q_hat * sigma_hat(x): WIDER where the model is unsure, TIGHTER where it is confident, while preserving marginal coverage. This directly fixes the audit complaint that 'confidence is capped LOW but never validated against realized coverage' — coverage is now the validated quantity and confidence becomes the (calibrated) width modulator. 

CONFORMALIZED QUANTILE REGRESSION (CQR — Romano, Patterson, Candes NeurIPS 2019), as a v2 upgrade path: instead of a symmetric +-q band, fit lower/upper quantile estimators q_lo, q_hi (we can derive cheap pinball-loss quantiles over the calibration residual distribution or use the meta-GBM's residual spread), conformity score E_i = max(q_lo(x_i) - y_i, y_i - q_hi(x_i)), interval = [q_lo(x) - Q_{1-alpha}(E), q_hi(x) + Q_{1-alpha}(E)]. CQR gives tighter, asymmetric, heteroskedastic-adaptive intervals with the same coverage guarantee — important because K is bounded in [0,1] and near the 0.85 gate the upper tail is compressed. 

MONDRIAN / GROUP-CONDITIONAL CONFORMAL (Vovk et al. 2003; MAPIE Mondrian): the autopilot serves MANY namespaces/bases with different K dynamics. Marginal coverage can be 90% overall while badly miscovering a single namespace. Mondrian CP partitions calibration rows into disjoint GROUPS (here: group = (namespace) or coarsely (basis: heuristic vs learned)) and computes a SEPARATE q_hat per group, giving GROUP-CONDITIONAL coverage. Falls back to the pooled quantile for groups below the small-n floor (honest 'insufficient_data per group', mirroring kscore-calibration.js's existing MIN_PAIRS_PER_CATEGORY contract). 

ADAPTIVE CONFORMAL INFERENCE (ACI — Gibbs & Candes NeurIPS 2021) for NON-STATIONARITY: the K-Score stream drifts (the whole point of the drift gate). Exchangeability is violated across drift. ACI keeps an ONLINE effective level alpha_t and updates it after each realized outcome: alpha_{t+1} = alpha_t + gamma*(alpha - err_t) where err_t = 1 if the last observed K fell OUTSIDE the interval we issued, else 0, and gamma is a small step (0.01-0.05). This drives the LONG-RUN empirical miscoverage to alpha even under arbitrary distribution shift, with no exchangeability assumption. The autopilot already records realized K per run in kscore-timeseries.js, which is exactly the feedback ACI needs. 

CONFORMAL SELECTIVE PREDICTION / ABSTENTION (SCoRE 2026; Selective Conformal Risk Control 2026; the judge-conformal paper 2509.18658 kolm already cites): with a calibrated interval [lo, hi] and the ship gate g=0.85, the autopilot decides: (a) lo >= g -> SHIP-SAFE: even the conformal LOWER bound clears the gate; the auto-deploy guardrail may EXECUTE. (b) hi < g -> SKIP-SAFE: even the upper bound misses the gate; do not compile/deploy. (c) lo < g <= hi -> ABSTAIN: the interval STRADDLES the gate; the decision is not statistically resolvable at this confidence — propose-only and request a human / acquire more data / reprompt the judge. This converts the deploy guardrail's eval_pass boolean and the simulator's point-estimate threshold into a RISK-CONTROLLED gate. 

DATA FLOW / PIPELINE PLACEMENT: backfillFromRuns (already exists) and kscore-timeseries already produce the (features, observed K) labelled rows. NEW src/conformal.js consumes those rows -> computes per-group q_hat (split + locally-weighted, optional CQR), maintains ACI alpha_t. quality-predictor.predictKScore calls conformal.conformalInterval(point=k, x=features, group=ns) to REPLACE its hand-drawn ci:[lo,hi] (returning the same {ci, confidence, basis} envelope shape, basis extended to 'conformal'|'conformal_localized'|'conformal_undercalibrated', plus a new optional coverage_target and decision hint). compile-simulator.simulateCompile, which currently compares two POINT estimates, additionally compares the conformal LOWER bound of (current+delta) vs the gate and emits a 'conformal_abstain' decision when straddling. autopilot-lifecycle._evaluateDeploy adds condition (6): conformal lower bound of the candidate K clears the gate (fail-closed). recordConformalOutcome(ns, observed_k, issued_interval) is called from the flywheel/deploy step to feed ACI and to grow the calibration pool. A scripts/bench-conformal-coverage.mjs harness simulates labelled streams and asserts realized coverage tracks the target — the validation the audit says is missing.
reference_impls: Angelopoulos & Bates, 'A Gentle Introduction to Conformal Prediction and Distribution-Free Uncertainty Quantification', arXiv:2107.07511 — canonical split-conformal regression recipe: score s=|y-yhat|, qhat = ceil((n+1)(1-alpha))/n empirical quantile, interval yhat +- qhat, coverage in [1-alpha, 1-alpha+1/(n+1)].
Romano, Patterson, Candes, 'Conformalized Quantile Regression (CQR)', NeurIPS 2019, arXiv:1905.03222 — asymmetric heteroskedastic-adaptive intervals; reference code github.com/yromano/cqr.

Lei, G'Sell, Rinaldo, Tibshirani, Wasserman, 'Distribution-Free Predictive Inference for Regression', JASA 2018 — split conformal + LOCALLY-WEIGHTED (normalized residual) variant s_i = |y_i - yhat_i| / sigma_hat(x_i).

Vovk, Lindsay, Nouretdinov, Gammerman, 'Mondrian Confidence Machines' (2003) + MAPIE Mondrian docs https://mapie.readthedocs.io/en/latest/theoretical_description_mondrian.html — group/class-conditional coverage by per-group calibration.

Gibbs & Candes, 'Adaptive Conformal Inference Under Distribution Shift', NeurIPS 2021, arXiv:2106.00170 — online alpha_{t+1}=alpha_t+gamma(alpha-err_t) for non-stationary streams. Follow-up arXiv:2208.08401 (JMLR 2024) tunes the step size.

Sheng, Liu, He, Zhao, Kang, 'Analyzing Uncertainty of LLM-as-a-Judge: Interval Evaluations with Conformal Prediction', EMNLP 2025, arXiv:2509.18658 / HF papers/2509.18658 — the SAME paper kolm's operator-kernels 'judge-conformal' entry already cites; midpoint-based score + reprompt policy. Wiring CP into quality-predictor ALIGNS kolm's own roadmap.

Xu, Guo, Wei, 'Selective Conformal Risk Control', arXiv:2512.12844 + 'Conformal Selective Prediction with General Risk Control (SCoRE)', arXiv:2603.24704 — abstain/defer threshold from a held-out calibration set; reject when interval straddles a decision boundary.

Tibshirani et al. class-conditional CP https://www.stat.berkeley.edu/~ryantibs/papers/classconf.pdf and Kandinsky CP arXiv:2502.17264 — overlapping/fractional group coverage (future generalization of Mondrian).

specs.benchmarks: Target: empirical marginal coverage within +-2 percentage points of nominal (90% target -> 0.88-0.92 realized) on a held-out simulated stream; group-conditional (Mondrian) coverage within +-3pp per namespace for n>=21; under an injected distribution shift, ACI long-run miscoverage converges to alpha within 200 steps (Gibbs-Candes report convergence to nominal under arbitrary shift). Width benchmark: locally-weighted/CQR intervals 10-30% narrower than fixed split-conformal at equal coverage (Romano 2019 report this on UCI regression). Abstention benchmark: on a labelled fixture where true K straddles the 0.85 gate within noise, the gate ABSTAINS instead of mis-deploying in >=95% of straddle cases (selective-CP decision-error control). specs.gap: Every autonomous autopilot decision that spends GPU/teacher money or promotes a model (simulateCompile compile/skip, cost-optimizer ΔK/$ ranking, _evaluateDeploy auto-promote) is gated on a POINT estimate from a predictor whose interval is, by its own admission, a fabricated confidence-scaled band with ZERO coverage guarantee, anchored to ONE real run (Trinity-500). There is (1) no distribution-free coverage guarantee, (2) no validation that the stated uncertainty matches realized error (the audit flags: 'confidence is capped LOW but never validated against realized coverage'), (3) no per-namespace (group-conditional) coverage so a single namespace can be silently mis-covered, (4) no handling of the non-stationary/drifting K stream the drift gate exists to catch, and (5) NO ABSTENTION mechanism — the system cannot say 'this decision is not statistically resolvable, ask a human', so it always takes a hard compile/skip/deploy decision even when the true K is genuinely uncertain around the 0.85 gate. An autonomous agent that deploys models on uncalibrated uncertainty is fragile in exactly the high-blast-radius path the w920 audit flagged as the single most safety-critical untested code path.

specs.risk: (1) Calibration-pool sparsity: most tenants have FAR fewer than MIN_CONFORMAL_CAL (21) labelled runs, so the conformal path rarely activates early and the heuristic band remains the live interval for a while — mitigated because the fallback is the EXISTING behavior (zero regression) and backfillFromRuns + kscore-timeseries accumulate the pool over time; the honest 'conformal_undercalibrated' basis tells callers when they are still on the band. (2) Exchangeability is violated by drift, which would break the marginal guarantee — mitigated by the ACI online level (designed precisely for arbitrary shift) and by Mondrian per-group calibration; document the residual assumption explicitly (kolm honesty-contract style). (3) Label quality: observed K used as the conformal target is itself an estimate from computeKScoreV2/eval artifacts; if those are biased the interval is calibrated to a biased target — mitigated by reusing the SAME computeKScoreV2 the rest of the autopilot already trusts, and by the coverage bench surfacing miscalibration. (4) The deploy-gate lower-bound condition is STRICTER than today, so some borderline candidates that previously auto-promoted will now ABSTAIN/propose-only — this is the intended safety improvement on the highest-blast-radius path, but flag it in release notes so it is not read as a regression. (5) Tiny-n quantile edge cases (b>n) must be handled or the interval silently over-tightens — covered by the undercalibrated guard + boundary unit tests.

specs.scope_note: READ-ONLY on product code. This is an execution-ready spec, no product edits were made. All file paths are absolute under C:/Users/user/Desktop/kolmogorov-stack.
specs.technique: Continual / lifelong learning with forgetting mitigation for the re-distilled student: a stratified experience-replay (rehearsal) mix as the PRIMARY lever, plus a Learning-without-Forgetting (LwF) champion-KD loss term as the data-free regularizer, with optional EWC (diagonal Fisher) and MoFO (momentum-filtered optimizer) as fallbacks when no prior corpus is available.
specs.surface_long: kolm's autopilot is, by design, an unbounded loop of re-distillation rounds. autopilot-daemon.tickAutopilot -> improvement-orchestrator.orchestrateImprovement increments self_improvement_round and re-trains the student; failure-analyst.analyzeFailures mints corrective fix-pairs each cycle (appendFixPairs, provenance.strategy='failure-fix') that bias round N+1's training mix toward the latest worst-cluster. Each round re-distills (distill-pipeline.distill -> workers/distill -> apps/trainer/distill.py) on a corpus dominated by the newest failures with NO retention of prior-round winning examples and NO regularization toward the deployed champion. compareAndDecide is the only guardrail and it only DETECTS regressions after the fact. This is a textbook catastrophic-forgetting setup; the technique converts the loop from a 'detect-and-rollback' open loop into a 'prevent-at-train-time' ratchet.

how_it_works.problem_statement: Sequential fine-tuning on a shifting data distribution (round-N failures, then round-(N+1) failures) drives the student's parameters toward the new task and away from prior-round optima. Catastrophic forgetting: capabilities that round N had can silently regress in round N+1 even when the new fix-pairs land. Empirically severe in LLM continual fine-tuning: SSR (arXiv:2403.01244) measured backward-transfer (BWT, the mean drop on earlier tasks; 0 = no forgetting) of -53.64 on 10 sequential SuperNI tasks for Llama-2-7b under naive sequential SFT. kolm is exactly this setup per-namespace, per-round.

three_families_and_kolm_choice.replay_rehearsal_PRIMARY: Mix a small stratified buffer of prior-round WINNING examples (the pairs that trained the current champion) back into the new round's training set. The single most effective LLM-continual-learning method in the literature and the cheapest to wire because kolm already persists every distill run's seeds.jsonl + augment-pairs.jsonl with provenance. SSR shows ~10% rehearsal ratio recovers BWT from -53.64 to -1.56 (10 tasks) and -42.39 to -0.79 (5 tasks), matching or beating real-data replay. When the prior corpus is genuinely unavailable, fall back to Self-Synthesized Rehearsal: the deployed champion regenerates synthetic prior-task instances (in-context few-shot from retained provenance prompts) and the latest student refines outputs.
three_families_and_kolm_choice.lwf_champion_kd_SECONDARY: Learning-without-Forgetting (Li & Hoiem, arXiv:1606.09282): add a knowledge-distillation term that keeps the new student's output distribution close to the DEPLOYED CHAMPION's distribution on the new round's inputs. Needs NO stored prior data -- the champion is the soft-target generator. Slots into kolm's existing combined loss with zero structural change (see pipeline_placement). Orthogonal to replay: replay protects on prior INPUTS, LwF protects FUNCTIONAL behavior on current inputs.
three_families_and_kolm_choice.ewc_optional_FALLBACK: Elastic Weight Consolidation (Kirkpatrick et al., arXiv:1612.00796): L2 penalty weighted by the diagonal Fisher information F_i toward the champion's parameters theta*. Literature consensus (the empirical study + meta-intelligence guide) is that EWC is WEAKER than replay for NLP/LLMs because parameter importance is more uniform across language tasks, limiting the discriminative power of the Fisher mask. Ship EWC behind a flag, default off; it is the right tool only for full-finetune (not LoRA) rounds where replay data is unavailable.
three_families_and_kolm_choice.mofo_optional_FALLBACK: Momentum-Filtered Optimizer (arXiv:2407.20999): a data-free, replay-free optimizer that down-weights the gradient coordinates whose update direction conflicts with accumulated momentum, preserving parameters important to prior knowledge. Cheapest possible option (no buffer, no champion forward pass, no Fisher) -- recommended as the zero-data fallback when neither prior corpus nor a loadable champion exists.

math.replay_mix: Training set R_{N+1} = NewPairs ∪ Sample(ChampionWinningPool, k) where k = round(rho * |NewPairs| / (1 - rho)) so replayed rows are a fraction rho of the final mix (default rho=0.10). Sampling is STRATIFIED by capture cluster (active-learning _bucketKey) to preserve the prior round's cluster coverage, not just its volume. Each replayed row keeps provenance.strategy='rehearsal' + source_round so receipts are auditable.
math.lwf_loss: L = (1-lambda_lwf)*L_task + lambda_lwf*L_lwf,  where L_task is kolm's current loss (alpha*L_kd_teacher + (1-alpha)*L_ce), and L_lwf = T_c^2 * KL( softmax(champion_logits/T_c) || softmax(student_logits/T_c) ) averaged over response positions. Defaults lambda_lwf=0.0 (OFF, byte-identical to baseline) -> recommended 0.3, T_c=2.0. Champion logits computed once per batch under torch.no_grad() exactly like the teacher pass already at distill.py:1085.
math.ewc_penalty: L_ewc = (lambda_ewc/2) * sum_i F_i * (theta_i - theta*_i)^2, F_i = diagonal Fisher estimated as the mean squared per-sample gradient of the champion's log-likelihood over a calibration subset (a few hundred prior-round winning rows). lambda_ewc default 0 (off); when on, typical 1e3-1e5 scaled to LoRA-param magnitude.
math.bwt_metric: Forgetting is quantified as per-class backward transfer: BWT_c = K_after(class c) - K_champion(class c), aggregated as mean over the champion's PASSING classes. The new continual-learning gate refuses promotion if mean negative BWT on previously-passing classes exceeds a budget (default 0.0 == zero net forgetting on what already worked).

data_flow_end_to_end: 1. CHAMPION POOL CAPTURE (new): when compareAndDecide promotes round N (writes promoted.json), a NEW hook snapshots that round's winning training rows into a per-(tenant,namespace) rehearsal buffer at ~/.kolm/rehearsal/<ns>.jsonl with {capture_id, input, output, cluster_key, source_round, provenance.strategy:'rehearsal'}. Bounded reservoir (default 5000 rows) with stratified-by-cluster eviction.
2. TICK / RE-DISTILL TRIGGER (existing): autopilot-daemon.tickAutopilot fires; improvement-orchestrator.orchestrateImprovement assembles candidates (new fix-pairs from failure-analyst).

3. MIX (new): buildRehearsalMix(tenant, namespace, new_pairs, {rho, strategy}) reads the rehearsal buffer, stratified-samples k rows, and returns the combined pairs + a rehearsal_manifest (counts per cluster, rho_realized). orchestrateImprovement threads this as pairs_override INTO distill().

4. TRAIN (modify): distill-pipeline.distill stamps continual_learning block into run-meta + spec.json and forwards lwf/ewc env to the worker. apps/trainer/distill.py compute_loss adds the LwF term (and optional EWC penalty) gated on lambda>0; the champion adapter path is passed via KOLM_LWF_CHAMPION_PATH.

5. EVALUATE + GATE (modify): compareAndDecide gains a continual block -- it already reads candidateRegressions vs baseRegressions; ADD a per-class BWT computation on the champion's previously-PASSING classes and a max_forgetting_budget that can force 'hold'/'rollback' even when net delta is positive (prevents a round that raises average K while quietly regressing a previously-solid class).

6. RECEIPT: run-meta.continual_learning + promoted.json.continual record rho_realized, lambda_lwf, replay_clusters, bwt_by_class so the autonomy audit trail can answer 'did this round forget anything, and what protected it?'
how_it_works.pipeline_placement: Three insertion points, all on existing chokepoints: (a) PROMOTE hook in improvement-orchestrator.compareAndDecide (snapshot champion pool) -- right where promoted.json is written; (b) MIX call in improvement-orchestrator.orchestrateImprovement before the distill() spawn, feeding pairs_override (distill already accepts pairs_override at distill-pipeline.js:~525 and fail-closed-filters holdout rows); (c) LOSS term in apps/trainer/distill.py _DistillTrainer.compute_loss at line ~1111 (loss = alpha*loss_kd + (1-alpha)*loss_ce) -- identical additive-loss pattern to the existing W828 reasoning_trace_loss_weight branch, which defaults to 0.0 and is documented as 'byte-identical to baseline'. This is the proven convention in-repo for adding an opt-in loss term without disturbing the default path.

reference_impls: Li & Hoiem, Learning without Forgetting (LwF), ECCV 2016 / TPAMI 2017 -- arXiv:1606.09282 ; code https://github.com/lizhitwo/LearningWithoutForgetting (the KD-against-old-outputs term kolm's LwF loss copies).
Kirkpatrick et al., Overcoming catastrophic forgetting in neural networks (EWC), PNAS 2017 -- arXiv:1612.00796 (diagonal-Fisher penalty).

Huang et al., Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting in LLMs with Self-Synthesized Rehearsal (SSR), ACL 2024 -- arXiv:2403.01244 ; html https://arxiv.org/html/2403.01244v1 (the no-stored-data rehearsal fallback; headline BWT numbers below).

Luo et al., An Empirical Study of Catastrophic Forgetting in LLMs During Continual Fine-tuning -- arXiv:2308.08747 (shows forgetting WORSENS with model scale 1B->7B; general-domain + reasoning degrade under sequential SFT).

Chen et al., Analyzing and Reducing Catastrophic Forgetting in PEFT / I-LoRA (dual-memory EMA over LoRA) -- arXiv:2402.18865 (up to 11% gains across 8 continual benchmarks; the EMA slow-learner is the optional 'champion-EMA' variant).
MoFO: Momentum-Filtered Optimizer for Mitigating Forgetting in LLM Fine-Tuning -- arXiv:2407.20999 (data-free, replay-free optimizer fallback).
Wang-ML-Lab, Continual Learning of LLMs: A Comprehensive Survey (CSUR 2025) -- https://github.com/Wang-ML-Lab/llm-continual-learning-survey (taxonomy + method index).
SuRe: Surprise-Driven Prioritised Replay for Continual LLM Learning -- arXiv:2511.22367 (fast/slow LoRA + EMA; informs prioritized-replay v2).
TRL SFTTrainer / DPOTrainer (already used at apps/trainer/trainer_real.py:171,291) -- the Trainer.compute_loss override path kolm already uses for KD.

benchmarks_to_target.primary_headline: Backward Transfer (BWT) on the autopilot's own previously-passing classes. Target: with rehearsal rho=0.10 + LwF lambda=0.3, mean BWT on previously-PASSING classes >= -0.02 K-Score (i.e. <=2 points net forgetting), vs naive sequential re-distill which the SSR study shows lands BWT ~ -0.40 to -0.53 normalized. SSR reference: non-rehearsal -53.64 -> SSR -1.56 (10 SuperNI tasks, Llama-2-7b); we report the kolm analog as per-class K-Score BWT.
benchmarks_to_target.secondary: Average K-Score across all classes (AR analog) must not REGRESS vs naive (SSR: AR 17.33 -> 63.23 on 10 tasks). kolm target: rehearsal+LwF average K >= naive average K (forgetting mitigation should be Pareto-non-inferior on the new task).
benchmarks_to_target.guardrail_metric: Promotion-blocked-on-forgetting events: the continual gate must catch >=1 synthetic 'ratchet-down' case in the test fixture where average K rises but a previously-passing class regresses by > budget.
benchmarks_to_target.cost: Replay adds ~rho extra rows (10% more steps). LwF adds one champion forward pass per batch (~+30-50% step time, same magnitude as the existing teacher pass). Both opt-in; default-off path is byte-identical to current.

kolm_current.re_distill_loop: src/autopilot-daemon.js tickAutopilot -> src/improvement-orchestrator.js orchestrateImprovement (increments self_improvement_round via _nextRoundForArtifact, builds run-meta.self_improvement, spawns distill OFFLINE via queueMicrotask). student_base defaults 'qwen-3b', use_curriculum default true. NO retention of prior-round winners; the new round trains on whatever pairs flow in.
kolm_current.fix_pair_minting: src/failure-analyst.js analyzeFailures clusters failing eval items by active-learning _bucketKey, ranks by fail_rate (tie-break n_failed), caps MAX_FIX_PAIRS=50 for the WORST cluster only, and writes corrective pairs via src/data-augment.js appendFixPairs (provenance.strategy='failure-fix'). This is the per-round distribution shift that drives forgetting.
kolm_current.gate: src/improvement-orchestrator.js compareAndDecide: delta = candidate_kscore - base_kscore; regressions = candidateRegressions.filter(c => !baseRegressions.includes(c)); promote when delta>=min_kscore_delta(0.02) AND regressions.length<=max_regression_classes(0); rollback when delta<-min_kscore_delta; else hold. This is DETECTION of new per-class regressions, computed only when the artifact manifest carries a regressions list -- it does not quantify magnitude of forgetting on previously-PASSING classes and never PREVENTS it at train time.
kolm_current.trainer_loss: apps/trainer/distill.py _DistillTrainer.compute_loss (~line 1073-1131): teacher forward under torch.no_grad() (line ~1085), then loss = alpha*loss_kd + (1.0-alpha)*loss_ce (line 1111), with an optional additive trace term gated on reasoning_trace_loss_weight>0 (default 0.0, explicitly 'byte-identical to the W713 baseline'). DistillConfig has alpha=0.9, temperature=2.0, forward/reverse/JSD objectives, top_k prune, on_policy. NO champion/previous-model reference, NO replay buffer read, NO Fisher/EWC, NO MoFO. grep of src + apps/trainer for ewc/fisher/lwf/rehearsal/replay-buffer/forgetting returns nothing (only 'regularization'/'regressions' as unrelated text).
kolm_current.data_paths: Each distill run persists seeds.jsonl + run-meta.json under ~/.kolm/distill-runs/<run_id>; augment pairs at the data-augment target path per namespace. promoted.json under ~/.kolm/registry/<artifact_id>/ records self_improvement_round + delta. The winning rows of a promoted round are RECOVERABLE (telemetry_seed capture_ids in run-meta + augment-pairs.jsonl) but are never snapshotted into a durable rehearsal buffer.

specs.gap: The autopilot's core premise (repeated re-distillation) has ZERO train-time forgetting mitigation. compareAndDecide only detects NEW per-class regressions (set difference) and only when a regressions list exists on the manifest; it cannot see magnitude, cannot see forgetting on classes that still nominally 'pass' but dropped, and never prevents the regression -- it can only roll back AFTER a bad round trained and (under auto-deploy) potentially shipped. There is no rehearsal buffer, no LwF/champion-KD term, no EWC/Fisher, no MoFO, and no BWT metric anywhere in src or apps/trainer. Without this, the improvement LOOP can ratchet DOWN: a round can raise average K while silently regressing a previously-solid capability, and the only safety net is a coarse rollback that wastes the entire training spend.

improvement.summary: Make the re-distill loop a RATCHET, not a loop, by (1) snapshotting each promoted round's winning rows into a durable stratified rehearsal buffer, (2) mixing rho% of that buffer back into every subsequent round (default-on at rho=0.10 once a buffer exists, with SSR fallback when it doesn't), (3) adding an opt-in LwF champion-KD loss term in the trainer (default lambda=0.0 == byte-identical baseline), (4) upgrading compareAndDecide with a quantified per-class BWT computation + a max_forgetting_budget that can force hold/rollback even on positive average delta, and (5) stamping a continual_learning receipt block on run-meta + promoted.json. EWC and MoFO ship behind flags (default off) as data-free fallbacks for full-finetune / no-buffer rounds.
improvement.phasing: Phase 1 (JS, low-risk, no GPU): rehearsal-buffer.js + buildRehearsalMix wiring + compareAndDecide BWT gate + receipts + tests with stubbed K-Scores. This alone closes the structural risk for the COMMON case (LoRA re-distill with recoverable prior rows) using only the replay lever, which the literature says is the strongest. Phase 2 (Python, GPU path): LwF champion-KD loss term in distill.py + champion-adapter plumbing; default-off so it never disturbs the green default path. Phase 3 (optional): EWC diagonal-Fisher + MoFO optimizer flags, SSR synthetic fallback.

specs.effort_breakdown: Phase 1 (rehearsal-buffer.js + buildRehearsalMix + computeBackwardTransfer + compareAndDecide gate + orchestrate wiring + JS tests + receipts): ~3.5 days. Phase 2 (LwF champion-KD in distill.py + champion plumbing through distill-pipeline + worker env + CPU pytest): ~2.5 days. Phase 3 (EWC + MoFO + SSR fallback, optional, flag-gated): ~1 day to scaffold and unit-test (full GPU validation deferred). Phase 1 alone closes the structural risk for the common LoRA re-distill case and is the recommended minimum ship.
specs.impact_rationale: Addresses a STRUCTURAL risk in the product's core premise (unbounded re-distillation). Today the only safety net is compareAndDecide's coarse new-regression detection + post-hoc rollback, which wastes the full training spend and cannot see magnitude or forgetting on still-'passing' classes. Converting the loop into a prevent-at-train-time ratchet (replay + LwF) plus a quantified forgetting gate is what lets the autopilot run autonomously for many rounds without silently degrading. It is also a roadmap-completeness win: NO continual-learning entry exists in any frontier doc for this surface. Ranked 'high' rather than 'critical' only because the immediately safety-critical fix on the auto-deploy path is the sequential-A/B candidate; this candidate is the structural-durability fix that makes long-horizon autonomy safe.
specs.risk: Medium-low. (1) The LwF/EWC GPU path requires a loadable champion and adds a champion forward pass per batch (~+30-50% step time) -- mitigated by default-off + lambda=0 byte-identity (same proven convention as the W828 trace-loss branch). (2) Replay can re-surface STALE prior answers if the canonical answer for a cluster has since changed; mitigated by stratified buffer eviction + source_round provenance + the option to refresh replayed outputs via SSR-style champion refinement (Phase 3). (3) Over-strong forgetting budget could block legitimate net-positive rounds; mitigated by making max_forgetting_budget configurable (default 0.0 = zero net forgetting on previously-passing classes, can be loosened per-namespace). (4) The continual gate needs per-class scores on the artifact manifest; when absent it must degrade gracefully to today's set-difference regression check (honest fallback, no crash). (5) EWC's known weakness on NLP (uniform parameter importance) means it should NOT be the headline lever -- it is correctly demoted to an optional fallback, with replay as primary per the literature.
specs.grok_corroboration: XAI_API_KEY was unset in this environment, so the Grok co-researcher call was skipped per the task's silent-skip instruction. All findings rest on primary sources verified via web search/fetch: SSR (arXiv:2403.01244, html fetched -- BWT -53.64->-1.56 / -42.39->-0.79 and ~10% rehearsal ratio confirmed), LwF (arXiv:1606.09282 + ECCV-2016 code repo), EWC (arXiv:1612.00796), the empirical-study finding that forgetting worsens with scale (arXiv:2308.08747), I-LoRA dual-memory EMA (arXiv:2402.18865), MoFO data-free optimizer (arXiv:2407.20999, fetched), and the LLM continual-learning survey (Wang-ML-Lab CSUR 2025). Cross-check consensus across these sources: experience replay/rehearsal is the strongest and simplest LLM-continual-learning lever, EWC is comparatively weak for NLP, and KD-against-previous-checkpoint (LwF) is the standard data-free regularizer -- which is exactly the primary/secondary ordering this spec recommends.

specs.technique: Data-scaling-law / Chinchilla-style data-budget model: fit a parametric law of K-Score (or fine-tune loss) vs n_pairs per task/namespace using the Rectified Scaling Law form (Lin et al. ICLR-track 2024, arXiv:2402.02314), then differentiate it to get marginal-quality-per-row, a budget-to-target estimate, and a principled stopping rule.
specs.surface: Autopilot / Autonomous Agent (decision-intelligence layer): quality prediction sub-system that feeds the cost-optimizer ingest-more / gap-fill deltas, the compile-simulator compile/skip decision, and the lifecycle data-acquisition stopping rule.
specs.how_it_works:
GOAL: replace the hand-tuned, log-saturating, single-anchor pairs heuristic with a DATA-DRIVEN scaling curve that the autopilot refits as runs accumulate, so it can answer 'how many MORE labeled pairs to reach target K, and is the next batch worth the spend?' with a defensible number instead of a fixed +500/+150 prior.

MODEL FORM (core): use the RECTIFIED SCALING LAW (arXiv:2402.02314, the only published law that captures the small-data 'pre-power phase' that IS kolm's regime — sub-1000 pairs). In loss space: L_hat(D) = B / (D_l + D^beta) + E, where D = n_pairs (fine-tune data size), B>0 sets the initial-loss scale, D_l>=0 is the 'pre-learned data size' (how much downstream signal the base model already carries from pretraining — large D_l => student starts already-good, curve is flat-then-bends; this term is what makes the law fit at small n where a vanilla power law fails), beta>0 is the learning-difficulty exponent (smaller beta => harder task, later phase transition), E>=0 is the irreducible loss at infinite data. kolm tracks K-Score in [0,1] (ship gate 0.85, higher=better), NOT loss. We bridge with a monotone link L = -ln(K) (pseudo-loss: K=1 -> L=0, K->0 -> L->inf, strictly decreasing), fit the law in L-space (where the power-law math holds), then map predictions back via K_hat(D) = exp(-L_hat(D)). The K-domain curve is K_hat(D) = exp(-(B/(D_l + D^beta) + E)), which is monotone-increasing and saturating in D — exactly the shape the heuristic hand-approximates, but now fitted.

FITTING (Hitchhiker's-Guide recipe, arXiv:2410.11840 + scipy-cookbook robust-regression practice): we have a set of observed (n_pairs_i, K_i) points per (tenant, namespace) from kscore-timeseries + backfillFromRuns. (1) Convert each to (D_i, L_i=-ln(clamp(K_i, 1e-3, 1-1e-3))). (2) Minimize HUBER loss (delta=1e-3) of the LOG-residual r_i = ln(L_hat(D_i)) - ln(L_i) over theta=(ln B, ln D_l, beta, ln E) (log-parameterize the positive params so the optimizer is unconstrained and scale-robust — standard scaling-law practice). (3) Optimize with a small pure-JS BFGS / Nelder-Mead from a GRID of initializations (the loss surface is non-convex; literature uses a grid of seeds and keeps the best) — e.g. beta in {0.05,0.1,0.2,0.4}, D_l in {0, n_min, mean(D)}, B/E from a closed-form linear seed. (4) Report fit residual RMSD; the rectified law reaches ~0.007 RMSD vs ~0.036 for the vanilla power law in the paper, so we gate 'trust the fit' on RMSD below a threshold AND n_points>=4 (the AtS algorithm fits the curve from as few as 3-4 size-loss pairs).

DERIVED QUANTITIES the autopilot consumes:
  - K_hat(D): point estimate of K at any data size D (drop-in for _pairsSubScore's role and for cost-optimizer's ingest-more/gap-fill projections, replacing fixed deltas with K_hat(D+delta)-K_hat(D)).
  - marginal_dk_per_row(D) = dK_hat/dD = K_hat(D) * (B*beta*D^(beta-1)) / (D_l+D^beta)^2  (analytic derivative; quality gained by the NEXT row at the current corpus size — the true economic signal).
  - pairs_to_target(K_target): solve K_hat(D)=K_target for D in closed form: let Lt=-ln(K_target); D_target = ((B/(Lt-E)) - D_l)^(1/beta) when Lt>E (target is achievable) else Infinity (target above the law's asymptote exp(-E) — surfaced as 'unreachable_with_this_data_axis', tells the autopilot to switch strategy, not buy more rows).
  - stopping rule (Accept-then-Stop adaptation): recommend STOP acquiring when marginal_dk_per_row(D) * expected_batch_rows < min_delta_k (the next batch can't clear the ship-gate delta) OR projected ingest-more cost-per-ΔK exceeds budget headroom. This is the missing 'stopping point' the discovery flagged.

DATA FLOW / PIPELINE PLACEMENT: scaling-law.js sits BESIDE quality-predictor.js (same autopilot intelligence layer). On each tick, autopilot-lifecycle.tickAutopilotFull already derives the feature vector and calls rankStrategies + simulateCompile. We insert: (a) fitDataScalingLaw({tenant, namespace}) reads (D,K) points from kscore-timeseries.getKScoreSeries (which already stores n_pairs alongside each point — small additive change to recordKScore to also persist n_pairs) plus quality-predictor.backfillFromRuns rows; returns a fitted {B,D_l,beta,E,rmsd,n_points,basis}. (b) quality-predictor gains a THIRD basis 'scaling_law' that fires when a trustworthy fit exists for this namespace (n_points>=4 && rmsd<=RMSD_GATE), sits ABOVE 'heuristic' but BELOW 'learned' in confidence, and uses K_hat(n_pairs) for the pairs contribution / overall K when n_pairs is the dominant moving feature. (c) cost-optimizer's ingest-more and gap-fill ΔK projections call marginal_dk_per_row / K_hat instead of the frozen STRATEGIES deltas when a fit exists. (d) lifecycle adds a data_budget block to the tick envelope: {pairs_to_target, marginal_dk_per_row, recommend_acquire|stop, projected_cost_to_target}. The fit is per-namespace, cached, and refit only when getKScoreSeries returns new points (cheap, pure-JS, deterministic). Cold start (n_points<4 or bad RMSD) => fall through to today's heuristic, with basis reported truthfully — same honesty contract as qp-v1.

specs.gap: kolm has NO data->quality curve model. It cannot answer the autopilot's central economic question precisely ('how many more pairs to reach target K, and is the next batch worth it?'). The pairs term is a fixed log-saturating shape anchored to ONE run with hand-tuned constants; it does not adapt as a namespace accumulates its OWN runs, cannot express the small-data pre-power phase (where adding pairs barely moves K until a phase transition), gives no marginal-ΔK-per-row, no closed-form budget-to-target, and no stopping rule. The cost-optimizer therefore ranks ingest-more / gap-fill on fixed priors that may be wildly off for a given corpus, and the lifecycle has no principled 'stop acquiring data' signal — it will keep recommending paid ingest even past the point of diminishing returns. None of this appears in the frontier docs (product-frontier-map.json / product-frontier-lab.json / operator-kernels / implementation-contracts) for the autopilot surface, so it is net-new to the roadmap.

reference_impls: Lin et al., 'Selecting Large Language Model to Fine-tune via Rectified Scaling Law', arXiv:2402.02314 (project: https://rectified-scaling-law.github.io/) — PRIMARY. Defines L_hat(D)=B/(D_l+D^beta)+E with the 'pre-learned data size' D_l that captures the small-data pre-power phase (kolm's exact regime); the 'Accept-then-Stop' (AtS) algorithm fits from as few as 3-4 halved-subset size-loss pairs; reported RMSD 0.007 (vs 0.036 vanilla power law), >=95% relative accuracy at 1/256 of full data, Pearson 0.666 vs 0.386 baseline under a 1/512 budget.

Hu et al. (ICLR 2024), 'When Scaling Meets LLM Finetuning: The Effect of Data, Model and Finetuning Method', arXiv:2402.17193 / OpenReview 5HCnKDeTws — the multiplicative joint law L(X,D_f)=A*X^-alpha*D_f^-beta+E; reports fine-tune-data exponent beta in 0.015-0.15 (full fine-tune more data-hungry than PEFT). Useful for sanity-bounding fitted beta and for the multi-factor extension (data x base-model-size).

Caballero et al. (ICLR 2023), 'Broken Neural Scaling Laws', arXiv:2210.14891 (code: https://github.com/ethancaballero/broken_neural_scaling_laws) — smoothly-broken power law with explicit break points; richer alternative if a single rectified law underfits a namespace with a plateau/inflection. Documented as the sl-v2 upgrade path, not sl-v1.
Hernandez et al., 'Scaling Laws for Transfer', arXiv:2102.01293 — formalizes 'effective data transferred' D_l-like pretraining contribution; theoretical backing for the D_l term meaning.

Choshen, Zhang, Andreas, 'A Hitchhiker's Guide to Scaling Law Estimation', arXiv:2410.11840 — practical fitting guidance: Huber loss on log-residuals + BFGS, grid of initializations, fit improves with intermediate points; basis for the _huberLogResidualLoss + grid-seed recipe.

SciPy Cookbook 'Robust nonlinear regression' (https://scipy-cookbook.readthedocs.io/items/robust_regression.html) + scipy.optimize.least_squares 'huber'/'soft_l1' loss — reference for the robust-loss + log-space-fit numerics our pure-JS implementation mirrors.
specs.benchmarks: Fit quality (held-out): RMSD of K_hat vs observed K on a leave-last-out split <= 0.05 on synthetic-from-known-law data (paper achieves 0.007 in loss-space). Extrapolation: given the first ceil(n/2) points, predict the last point's K within +/-0.05 absolute on >=80% of synthetic namespaces. Sample efficiency: a usable fit (basis:'rectified', rmsd<=gate) from as few as 4 (n_pairs,K) points (matches AtS k>=3). Calibration of pairsToTarget: predicted pairs_to_target within 1.5x of the true crossing on synthetic data. Decision lift vs incumbent: on a synthetic corpus where the true law has a sharp pre-power phase, the scaling-law basis' ingest-more ΔK projection must be closer to the true ΔK than the frozen +500-prior heuristic in >=70% of cases. Determinism: identical points + seed grid => identical params (bit-stable), matching kolm-meta-trainer's determinism contract.

specs.risk: Over-confident extrapolation from a non-convex fit on few/noisy points is the central risk — a bad fit could tell the autopilot to keep buying data (or to stop too early). MITIGATIONS: (a) gate the 'scaling_law' basis on n_points>=4 AND rmsd<=RMSD_GATE, falling through to the honest heuristic otherwise; (b) keep scaling-law output ADVISORY to data acquisition only — it must NOT become a new autonomous-deploy trigger (the 5-condition guardrail in autopilot-lifecycle._evaluateDeploy is untouched); (c) cap scaling_law confidence below the learned path so a fit never out-trusts the GBM; (d) grid-of-initializations + Huber loss to avoid local minima and outlier capture; (e) clamp pairsToTarget and reject targets above achievable_k_max (exp(-E)) with an explicit 'switch_strategy' signal rather than returning a huge buy-more number. SECONDARY risk: per-namespace fits need enough runs to be useful — cold namespaces simply stay on the heuristic (no regression). NUMERIC risk: pure-JS Nelder-Mead must be tested for determinism/stability; mitigated by fixed seed grid + iteration cap + log-parameterization. SCOPE risk: resist extending to the full multiplicative joint law (data x model-size x method) in sl-v1 — that is the documented sl-v2 path.

specs.technique: Multi-armed bandit strategy selection — budgeted, non-stationary Thompson sampling (Normal-Gamma posterior with discounting + warm-start priors) over the 5 improvement strategies, with cost-aware reward/cost-ratio arm selection
specs.surface: Autopilot / Autonomous Agent — the improvement-loop decision layer (src/cost-optimizer.js rankStrategies, invoked once per cron tick by src/autopilot-lifecycle.js tickAutopilotFull -> recommended strategy -> simulateCompile -> re-distill)
specs.how_it_works:
PROBLEM FRAMING. Each autopilot tick must answer: which of the 5 improvement strategies (dedup / ingest-more / gap-fill / preference / evol) buys the most K-Score per dollar on THIS namespace's corpus, right now? Today this is a one-shot greedy argmax on a FIXED predicted ratio (predicted_delta_k / max(cost, eps)) using frozen characteristic-prior feature deltas — it never learns which strategy actually paid off, never explores under uncertainty, and clamps regressions to 0. This is exactly a BUDGETED, NON-STATIONARY multi-armed bandit: arms = strategies; reward = realized post-compile DeltaK; cost = teacher-token spend; reward distribution drifts because the corpus state changes every round (dedup stops paying once the corpus is clean; ingest-more saturates per the data-scaling law). Thompson sampling is the textbook policy: it has near-optimal Bayesian regret, adapts exploration to data without hand-tuned schedules, and self-corrects instantly when an arm degrades (Stitch Fix's stated reason for choosing TS in production).

MODEL. Reward per strategy a is the realized DeltaK (continuous, can be negative), so the conjugate model is a Normal-Gamma (Normal-Inverse-Gamma) per arm: unknown mean mu_a and unknown precision tau_a. Hyperparameters per arm: (mu0_a, kappa_a, alpha_a, beta_a). Posterior after observing rewards with discounted effective count n_eff, discounted mean xbar, discounted sum-of-squares S:
  kappa_n  = kappa0 + n_eff
  mu_n     = (kappa0*mu0 + n_eff*xbar) / kappa_n
  alpha_n  = alpha0 + n_eff/2
  beta_n   = beta0 + 0.5*S + 0.5*(kappa0*n_eff*(xbar-mu0)^2)/kappa_n
SAMPLING one posterior draw of the mean reward for arm a:
  tau ~ Gamma(shape=alpha_n, rate=beta_n)            (precision)
  mu_tilde ~ Normal(mu_n, 1/(kappa_n * tau))          (mean reward draw)
This is the standard NIG posterior-predictive-mean sampler (van den Burg; Honda & Takemura 2014).

NON-STATIONARITY (DISCOUNTED TS). Because reward distributions drift round-to-round, raw counts over-trust stale rewards. We apply a discount gamma in (0,1] (Qi et al. 2023, Discounted Thompson Sampling): when folding the j-th most recent reward r_j (j=0 newest), weight it by gamma^j. So n_eff = sum_j gamma^j, xbar = (sum_j gamma^j * r_j)/n_eff, S = sum_j gamma^j*(r_j - xbar)^2. Equivalently maintain decaying sufficient statistics incrementally: on each new reward, multiply the running (count, sum, sumsq) by gamma then add the new point. Recommended gamma ~ 0.9 (forget over ~10 effective rounds; tunable). gamma=1 recovers stationary TS. A sliding-window variant (Beta-SWTS / gamma-SWGTS, Fiandri et al. 2024) is the alternative; discounting is chosen here because it needs no window buffer and folds into a single incremental update.

BUDGETED / COST-AWARE SELECTION (Xia et al. IJCAI 2015, Budgeted Thompson Sampling). The autopilot optimizes DeltaK PER DOLLAR, not raw DeltaK, so we do not pick argmax of the sampled mean — we pick argmax of the sampled-reward-over-cost RATIO. Cost is also a (lower-variance, near-deterministic) posterior: model est_cost_usd per arm with a small Gamma posterior (or, since cost is well-modeled analytically by estimateBatchCost, use the analytic point cost as a near-deterministic draw and only Bayes-track the realized/estimated cost discrepancy). Per tick: for each arm draw mu_tilde_a (NIG) and cost_tilde_a; compute ratio_a = mu_tilde_a / max(cost_tilde_a, EPSILON); recommend argmax ratio_a among budget-feasible arms. dedup (cost=0) keeps its today-behavior of floating to the top whenever its sampled reward is positive — preserved exactly.

WARM-START / COLD-START PRIOR (Empirical-Bayes / Dynamic-Prior TS). The hard part of any bandit on a fresh namespace is the n=0 cold start, which is precisely kolm's honesty theme. Rather than an uninformative prior (which over-explores), we WARM-START each arm's prior mean mu0_a from the EXISTING frozen characteristic delta: project STRATEGIES[a].delta through predictKScore on the current features to get prior_delta_k_a (the exact number rankStrategies computes today), and set mu0_a = prior_delta_k_a with a WEAK prior strength kappa0 (e.g. kappa0=1, alpha0=1, beta0 tuned so prior variance ~ (0.1)^2). Result: at zero observed rewards the sampled mean concentrates near today's heuristic ranking (graceful degradation to current behavior — no regression risk on day 0), and as realized rewards accumulate the data overrides the prior. This is the bandit analog of the predictor's 'low capped confidence' contract.

DATA FLOW / PIPELINE PLACEMENT. (1) SELECT: tickAutopilotFull calls rankStrategies, which now (behind a flag) routes through the bandit: read the per-(tenant,namespace,strategy) discounted posterior from the strategy-outcome ledger, warm-start any unseen arm from the characteristic prior, draw one sample per arm, rank by sampled ratio, recommend. The returned envelope keeps the co-v1 shape and adds posterior diagnostics (posterior_mean, sampled_ratio, n_eff, prior_warm_started). (2) ACT: the recommended strategy feeds simulateCompile -> (if compile) re-distill. The CHOSEN strategy + base K + base features are written to a new strategy-outcome ledger row (status 'pending'). (3) OBSERVE: when the re-distill completes and a fresh candidate K is recorded (the existing flywheel hook in tickAutopilotFull step 8 -> kscore-timeseries.recordKScore), a new function recordStrategyOutcome ties realized DeltaK = candidate_K - base_K back to the pending row and appends it as a bandit reward. (4) LEARN: the next tick's rankStrategies reads the updated discounted posterior. The whole loop reuses the event-store (one new provider tag) and the existing kscore-timeseries reward signal — no new datastore. Reward feed: realized DeltaK from compareAndDecide / kscore-timeseries; cost feed: estimateBatchCost (already used).
specs.kolm_current: src/cost-optimizer.js rankStrategies (co-v1) is a GREEDY one-shot policy. STRATEGIES is an Object.freeze of 5 fixed feature-delta priors (lines 106-141): dedup {dup_fraction:-0.10, n_pairs:-20, cost 0}, ingest-more {n_pairs:+500}, gap-fill {coverage:+0.15,n_pairs:+150}, preference {avg_quality:+0.08}, evol {avg_quality:+0.05,coverage:+0.05}. For each it _applyDelta (line 185), calls predictKScore on base and projected vectors, differences to predicted_delta_k, prices via estimateBatchCost, and sorts DESC by delta_k_per_dollar = predicted_delta_k / max(est_cost_usd, 1e-6) (lines 296, 314-318). Negative DeltaK is clamped to 0 (line 293 'Math.max(0, deltaK)') so a regressing strategy is scored as neutral and a no-op. recommended = first budget-feasible arm with predicted_delta_k>0 (lines 323-326). There is NO state across ticks: the same features always yield the same ranking; no posterior, no exploration, no realized-reward feedback. src/autopilot-lifecycle.js tickAutopilotFull (line 312) invokes rankStrategies (line 349), maps recommended -> STRATEGIES[recommended].delta -> simulateCompile (line 365), and on a fresh candidate K calls kscore-timeseries.recordKScore (line 414) — but that K is never linked back to the strategy that produced it. Grep of src for Thompson/bandit/UCB/epsilon-greedy/posterior-sampling/regret/strategy_outcome returns NOTHING (verified). The closest existing stats code is src/stat-sig.js (Welch t, fixed-horizon) and src/kolm-meta-trainer.js (toy stump GBM) — neither does online exploration. No strategy-outcome ledger exists. The audit (audit/w920/autopilot.audit.json) flags rankStrategies as untested and notes the negative-DeltaK clamp 'can mask a strategy that would actually regress quality'.
specs.gap: kolm's improvement loop is OPEN-LOOP and STATIC: (1) NO learning — rankStrategies never updates from realized outcomes, so a strategy whose characteristic prior is wrong for this namespace (e.g. evol pays nothing on an already-diverse corpus) keeps getting recommended forever; the loop cannot discover that ingest-more actually out-yields gap-fill HERE. (2) NO exploration — pure greedy argmax means a strategy that looked weak on the cold prior is never tried, even though one trial could reveal it as the best arm (no regret bound). (3) NON-STATIONARITY ignored — dedup's true reward collapses to ~0 once the corpus is deduped, but the frozen prior keeps promising dup_fraction:-0.10 every tick; nothing forgets stale yield. (4) REGRESSION MASKING — clamping DeltaK<0 to 0 means a strategy that genuinely lowers K is scored neutral, never penalized; a learning posterior would push its mean negative and stop selecting it. (5) NO UNCERTAINTY in selection — the ratio is a point estimate; the system cannot distinguish 'high-yield, high-confidence' from 'high-yield, never-tried'. Net: the autopilot is a fixed rule engine wearing the costume of an optimizer. Competitors framing themselves as 'self-improving' need an actual closed-loop policy with bounded regret; this is the single highest-leverage decision-intelligence upgrade on the surface and shares its reward feed with the sequential-A/B and conformal candidates.
specs.improvement: Add a budgeted, discounted, warm-started Thompson-sampling policy as the strategy selector, wired into rankStrategies behind an opt-in flag (use_bandit, default true once tests green; falls back to today's greedy path on any error so the autopilot never regresses). Build a new src/strategy-bandit.js owning the Normal-Gamma-per-arm posterior with exponential discounting (gamma), warm-started from the existing characteristic-prior DeltaK so n=0 behavior == today's behavior. Add a strategy-outcome ledger (new event-store provider tag 'kolm_strategy_bandit') with two functions: recordStrategyChoice (writes the 'pending' row at SELECT time with base_k + chosen strategy) and recordStrategyOutcome (folds realized DeltaK = candidate_K - base_K into the discounted posterior at OBSERVE time). rankStrategies, when use_bandit, replaces the deterministic delta_k_per_dollar sort with: read posteriors -> warm-start unseen arms -> draw one (reward,cost) sample per arm -> rank by sampled ratio -> recommend argmax feasible arm; it ALSO calls recordStrategyChoice for the recommended arm. tickAutopilotFull's flywheel step (line ~409) additionally calls recordStrategyOutcome once a fresh candidate K is known, closing the loop. The bandit NEVER clamps reward to 0 (negatives are real evidence). Cost is the near-deterministic estimateBatchCost value treated as a degenerate posterior draw (Bayes-track realized-vs-estimated only if a realized cost is later supplied). Everything is tenant+namespace fenced (W411 pattern) and returns the co-v1 envelope unchanged plus additive diagnostics.

MATH/PURE: (1) _normalGammaUpdate with gamma=1 matches closed-form NIG posterior for a hand-checked reward set (mu_n, kappa_n, alpha_n, beta_n within 1e-9). (2) gamma<1 downweights: feeding the same reward stream with gamma=0.5 yields strictly smaller n_eff than gamma=1, and a recent reward shift moves mu_n faster under gamma=0.5. (3) _sampleGamma mean ~= shape/rate and _sampleNormal mean/var match over 50k seeded draws (within 3%). POLICY: (4) WARM-START: rankByThompson at n_obs=0 for all arms, with priors set from the current greedy predicted_delta_k, recommends the SAME arm as today's greedy rankStrategies on >=95% of seeds (graceful degradation / no day-0 regression). (5) CONVERGENCE: simulate a fixed true reward per arm (e.g. ingest-more best), feed realized outcomes via recordStrategyOutcome for ~50 ticks; assert recommendation share for the true-best arm exceeds 80% in the last 10 ticks and cumulative regret grows sublinearly (compare against a greedy-on-stale-prior baseline that never converges). (6) NON-STATIONARITY: best arm switches at tick 25 (dedup pays then collapses to 0, ingest-more takes over); assert gamma=0.9 re-converges to the new best within ~10 ticks while gamma=1 lags. (7) REGRESSION HANDLING: an arm with true reward -0.05 has its posterior mean driven negative and its recommendation share -> ~0 (the today-clamp masking is gone). (8) COST-AWARE: with dedup cost=0 and positive sampled reward, dedup is recommended over a higher-raw-reward paid arm (ratio dominance preserved). (9) BUDGET: an arm whose est_cost_usd>budget is marked fits_budget:false and never recommended. specs.risk: MEDIUM. (1) Stochastic non-determinism: TS draws are random, so CI must use a seeded RNG (opts.rng) and assert on aggregate behavior (recommendation share, regret slope) over many seeds, not a single deterministic pick — otherwise flaky tests. Mitigation: the rng seam + share/slope assertions in the test plan. (2) Cold-start over-exploration if the warm-start prior is too weak: a fresh namespace could waste an early tick on a low-yield arm. Mitigation: kappa0 strong enough that n=0 == greedy (acceptance criterion #2); exploration only kicks in once a couple of real outcomes exist. (3) Reward latency / attribution: realized DeltaK only arrives after a re-distill completes (hours), so the posterior updates lag the choice — this is the SAME batch-staleness Stitch Fix accepts; the pending-row + choice_id matching handles it, and the autopilot tick cadence (cron, not per-request) tolerates it. (4) Sparse rewards: with only 5 arms and few rounds per namespace, posteriors stay wide for a while — acceptable because warm-start keeps behavior sane meanwhile; the bandit is strictly additive value once a handful of outcomes land. (5) Reward sign/scale: DeltaK can be tiny; ensure the cost normalization (ratio) and prior_beta scale are calibrated so sampled ratios are well-conditioned (EPSILON floor reused from co-v1). LOW residual risk because the greedy fallback guarantees the autopilot can never do WORSE than today.
