specs.technique: Sequential / always-valid A/B testing (GAVI confidence sequence + mSPRT always-valid p-value) to replace the fixed-horizon Welch t-test gate that the autopilot peeks every cron tick
specs.co_researcher: XAI_API_KEY was UNSET in this environment, so the Grok co-researcher leg was skipped silently per the task contract. All findings below are from primary sources (arXiv papers + production-engineering blogs) verified via WebSearch/WebFetch. grok_corroboration records this.
specs.how_it_works:
PROBLEM (statistics). A fixed-horizon test (Student/Welch t-test) controls the Type-I error rate alpha ONLY if you look at the data exactly once, at a pre-committed sample size. The kolm autopilot is the textbook anti-pattern: src/autopilot-daemon.js says the tick is 'cron-driven POST /v1/autopilot/tick every minute (or whatever cadence the operator picks)', and on each tick the A/B path (src/ab-router.js autoRollback -> stat-sig.gate) and the deploy path (src/autopilot-lifecycle.js _evaluateDeploy -> compareAndDecide) re-evaluate the SAME accumulating samples. Re-applying a fixed-horizon p<0.05 rule at every one of N peeks inflates the realized false-positive rate toward ~1 as N grows (Johari-Pekelis-Walsh show ~5x at modest N; with minutely cron over days, the gate is effectively guaranteed to fire a false 'promote' or false 'rollback' eventually). This is a correctness bug on the highest-blast-radius path (autonomous model promotion).

FIX (anytime-valid inference). An always-valid / anytime-valid procedure produces a test statistic that is valid at EVERY sample size simultaneously, so you may peek (and stop, promote, or roll back) at any time, any number of times, for any reason, while still controlling Type-I error at alpha. Two production-proven, closed-form, dependency-free families fit kolm's continuous K-Score-mean comparison:

(A) mSPRT (mixture Sequential Probability Ratio Test; Robbins 1970; Johari et al. 2019; the method Statsig ships). Define the mixture likelihood ratio of the true effect theta vs theta0=0 under a normal mixing prior N(0, tau^2) on theta. For two-sample normal data the mixture LR has a CLOSED FORM (verified verbatim from the canonical R implementation): given n paired observations, running means mu_a_n and mu_b_n, pooled variance V_n, and mixing variance tau_sq:
    Lambda_n = sqrt( V_n / (V_n + n*tau_sq) ) * exp( ( n^2 * tau_sq * (mu_a_n - mu_b_n)^2 ) / ( 2 * V_n * (V_n + n*tau_sq) ) )
The ALWAYS-VALID p-value is the running infimum of the reciprocal: p_0 = 1; p_n = min(p_{n-1}, 1/Lambda_n). Ville's inequality guarantees P(inf_n p_n <= alpha) <= alpha under H0, so 'reject (B beats A) the first tick p_n < alpha' controls Type-I error under unlimited peeking. tau_sq is the only tuning knob; convention (Johari) is tau_sq on the order of the outcome variance / the squared minimum-effect-of-interest (Statsig sets it from the MDE; we anchor it to DEFAULT_MIN_EFFECT_SIZE^2 with a variance fallback). p-value monotone-decreasing means the decision is STICKY: once significant, stays significant -> peeking-safe by construction.

(B) GAVI confidence sequence (Howard-Ramdas-Sekhon-McAuliffe 2021 'Time-uniform, nonparametric, nonasymptotic confidence sequences', Annals of Statistics 49(2):1055-1080; the method Eppo ships as its default). A confidence sequence (CS) is a sequence of intervals (L_t, U_t) with the time-UNIFORM guarantee P(forall t>=1 : Delta in (L_t,U_t)) >= 1-alpha (vs a fixed-horizon CI which only covers at one t). For the difference of means Delta = mu_b - mu_a with cumulative sample size t and per-observation standard-error estimate sigma_hat_Delta, the closed-form half-width (verbatim from Eppo's production docs) is:
    B = (1/sqrt(t)) * sqrt( (t + rho) * log( (t + rho) / (rho * (1-c)^2) ) )
    interval = mu_hat_Delta +/- sigma_hat_Delta * B
where c = 1-alpha (the coverage) and rho is the single tuning parameter set to minimize width near a target sample size N_tune:
    rho = N_tune / ( log( log( e / (1-c)^2 ) ) - 2*log(1-c) )
Eppo uses N_tune=10000. DECISION via the CS: promote arm B iff the CS lower bound clears the effect threshold (L_t > min_effect_size); roll back iff the CS upper bound is below it (U_t < min_effect_size, i.e. B is significantly NOT better / worse); otherwise 'continue' (keep sampling). The CS also yields a calibrated, anytime-valid interval the dashboard can render directly, and it is variance-adaptive.

WHICH AND WHY. We implement BOTH as pure functions and expose mSPRT as the default decision (matches kolm's existing p-value-shaped gate() contract and Statsig's production choice for streaming/continuous data) while the GAVI CS supplies the anytime-valid INTERVAL (replacing stat-sig.js's fake 95%-CI that uses a normal 1.96 quantile with NO peeking correction) and is the natural feed for the conformal-uncertainty candidate (w921 sibling). Group Sequential Testing (GST; Netflix/Spotify) is deliberately NOT chosen: GST is more powerful but REQUIRES a pre-committed max sample size and a finite (<=~100) peek schedule -- incompatible with an open-ended minutely cron that never knows its horizon. The autopilot's unbounded, streaming, schedule-free peeking is exactly the regime where mSPRT/GAVI dominate and GST cannot be applied.

DATA FLOW / PIPELINE PLACEMENT. Nothing new is captured. The realized-reward feed already exists: ab-router.recordOutcome writes per-arm kscore outcomes to the event-store (workflow w777:ab), readSamples pulls arm_a[]/arm_b[] back, and kscore-timeseries.recordKScore logs the candidate-vs-base K stream. Today stat-sig.gate(samples_a, samples_b) runs Welch on those arrays. The change inserts a new sequentialGate({samples_a, samples_b, method}) alongside welchT/gate in src/stat-sig.js, feeds the SAME arrays, and returns {decision:'promote'|'rollback'|'continue', avp, cs_low, cs_high, n}. ab-router.autoRollback and a new ab-router.sequentialDecision call it instead of (or in addition to) the fixed-horizon gate. On the deploy path, autopilot-lifecycle._evaluateDeploy gains a 6th condition: when an A/B test id is in scope, the sequential decision must be 'promote' (the anytime-valid analogue of the existing compareAndDecide K-delta check) before --auto can EXECUTE, closing the peeking hole on the autonomous promoter.
specs.kolm_current: src/stat-sig.js (STAT_SIG_VERSION 'w778-v1') is a FIXED-HORIZON Welch t-test. welchT() computes a two-sample Welch-Satterthwaite t with a hand-rolled regularized incomplete beta (Lentz continued fraction) + Lanczos log-gamma for the t-CDF tail. gate() layers a min_n=30 sample floor (DEFAULT_MIN_N), p<alpha (DEFAULT_ALPHA=0.05), and a min_effect_size=0.01 (DEFAULT_MIN_EFFECT_SIZE) check, returning decision 'pass'|'fail'|'insufficient'. The 95% CI it reports (stat-sig.js:238) uses a literal normal quantile tq = df>=30 ? 1.96 : 2.045 and is explicitly 'informational; the decision uses p directly' -- it has NO peeking correction. CALLERS that peek continuously: (1) src/ab-router.js autoRollback() (line ~588) does `await ss.gate({tenant, ab_test_id})` on every invocation and rolls back/holds on the fixed-horizon decision; getAbStatus() (line ~386) surfaces welchT to the dashboard/CLI each read. (2) The HTTP surface GET /v1/stat-sig/gate (src/router.js:24783) and POST /v1/ab-tests/:id/rollback (src/router.js:24696) call gate()/autoRollback() with no notion of how many prior peeks occurred. (3) cli/kolm.js cmdW778StatSig (dispatch 'stat-sig' at cli/kolm.js:40445; handler ~16911) exposes `kolm stat-sig gate --ab-test-id <id>` which an operator (or a cron loop) can hammer. (4) src/autopilot-daemon.js header (~389) states the tick is cron-driven 'every minute', and src/autopilot-lifecycle.js _evaluateDeploy (line 140) re-runs compareAndDecide (a fixed-horizon K-delta >= min_kscore_delta check, NOT even a p-value) on every --auto tick within the 48h grace window -- the autonomous-deploy decision is peeked repeatedly with zero sequential correction. GREP CONFIRMS ABSENCE: no mSPRT / e-value / always-valid / confidence-sequence / alpha-spending / group-sequential / SPRT code anywhere under src/ (only unrelated substring hits in names-list.js / deploy-config.js). specs.gap: The autopilot evaluates a FIXED-HORIZON significance test on continuously-accumulating, repeatedly-peeked samples. This silently inflates the Type-I error rate from the nominal 0.05 toward ~1 as the number of cron peeks grows: over minutely ticks across days/weeks the gate is effectively guaranteed to eventually emit a spurious 'promote' (ship a non-better or worse model) or a spurious 'rollback' (revert a genuinely-better model). It is not a quality nit -- it is a statistical correctness defect on the single highest-blast-radius path in the product (autonomous model promotion). Secondary gaps: (a) the reported 95% CI uses a normal 1.96 quantile with no peeking correction, so the interval the dashboard shows does not have its stated coverage under continuous monitoring; (b) there is no early-stop -> the system cannot promote the instant evidence is decisive nor stop a clearly-losing arm, wasting traffic/cost; (c) min_n=30 is an arbitrary fixed floor, not a principled stopping rule. The fix is well-trodden in industry -- Statsig (mSPRT), Eppo (GAVI), Netflix/Spotify (GST) all ship anytime-valid procedures specifically to make peeking safe -- and kolm, which advertises an opt-in autonomous improvement loop, currently ships the unsafe fixed-horizon version of exactly this gate.

specs.benchmarks: Primary acceptance is a SIMULATED A/A Type-I-error guarantee (the only meaningful benchmark for a peeking-correctness fix): under H0 (both arms drawn from the same distribution) with continuous peeking at every sample step up to N=5000, the realized false-positive rate of sequentialGate('msprt') and ('gavi') must be <= alpha (target 0.05) with a tolerance band that accounts for Monte-Carlo noise over >=2000 A/A replications (so <= ~0.065 upper). The fixed-horizon gate() on the same A/A peeking stream should demonstrably BLOW PAST alpha (>0.30 by N=5000) -- this contrast is the proof the fix matters. Statsig's published bar is the reference: 'FPR in A/A tests remaining below 5% even with repeated measurements over a 4-week period'. Secondary: under H1 with a true effect == 2*min_effect_size, sequentialGate must reach decision 'promote' (early-stop) at a median sample size strictly LESS than the fixed-horizon test's required n at the same power (the whole point of sequential = fewer samples to a decision), while never crossing on A/A. GAVI CS coverage: across the A/A replications, P(0 in CS for all t) >= 1-alpha (time-uniform coverage holds).

specs.risk: (1) CORRECTNESS-OF-FIX: the closed-form mSPRT exp() can overflow for large n*tau_sq -> MUST compute the log-mixture-LR in log space and take avp = exp(-logLambda) clamped to [0,1]; the GAVI log(log(...)) term requires (1-c)^2 < 1 (always true for alpha<1) but guard alpha in (0,1). Mitigation: pure-math unit tests on hand-worked fixtures (#3,#7) + the A/A simulation (#4,#8) catch any numeric or formula transcription error. (2) BEHAVIORAL CHANGE on a live path: routing autoRollback + the deploy guardrail through the new gate changes when promotions/rollbacks fire. Mitigation: env flag KOLM_AB_SEQUENTIAL (default ON but instantly revertible to 'off' = exact legacy behavior) + the deploy condition is purely ADDITIVE (it can only make --auto MORE conservative, never auto-promote something the legacy K-delta gate would have blocked) so the change is fail-safe in the dangerous direction. (3) TUNING SENSITIVITY: mSPRT tau_sq and GAVI n_tune affect POWER (time-to-decision), not validity (Type-I error is controlled for ANY tau_sq/n_tune per the theory and the Spotify writeup) -- so a bad tuning choice only slows decisions, it cannot break the guarantee; defaults anchor tau_sq to min_effect_size^2 and n_tune to Eppo's 10000. (4) The early-stop interacts with the existing min_n=30 floor and the 48h grace clock; keep both as independent guards (sequential significance AND min_n AND grace must all hold) so the autonomous deploy never fires on a tiny sample even if the avp dips early.
specs.grok_corroboration: Grok co-researcher SKIPPED: XAI_API_KEY is unset in this environment (verified `test -n $XAI_API_KEY` -> NOT set), so per the task's silent-skip contract no Grok call was made. All corroboration is from primary sources cross-checked across multiple independent production implementations: the same two closed-form methods recur across four major experimentation platforms -- Statsig ships mSPRT (Johari et al.), Eppo ships GAVI (Howard et al. 2021), Netflix + Spotify ship GST -- which independently confirms (a) the peeking-inflation problem is real and industry-recognized and (b) mSPRT/GAVI are the correct fit for kolm's open-horizon streaming regime while GST is not (GST needs a pre-committed max sample size, which a minutely cron with no horizon cannot supply). The exact GAVI half-width B = (1/sqrt(t))*sqrt((t+rho)*log((t+rho)/(rho*(1-c)^2))) and rho = N_tune/(log(log(e/(1-c)^2)) - 2*log(1-c)) were extracted verbatim from Eppo's production docs and are consistent with Howard et al. (2021); the mSPRT closed form Lambda_n = sqrt(V_n/(V_n+n*tau_sq))*exp(n^2*tau_sq*(mu_a-mu_b)^2/(2*V_n*(V_n+n*tau_sq))) with the running-min avp recurrence p_n=min(p_{n-1},1/Lambda_n) was extracted verbatim from the canonical R reference implementation and matches the Johari et al. statement and Ville's-inequality guarantee. No source contradicted another; the only divergence is platform CHOICE (mSPRT vs GAVI vs GST), which is driven entirely by data-arrival regime (streaming vs daily-batch vs known-horizon) -- kolm's regime selects mSPRT/GAVI, as argued.

specs.technique: DistiLLM-2: contrastive asymmetric SKL+SRKL distillation with on/off-policy curriculum
specs.how_it_works:
DistiLLM-2 (Ko et al., ICML 2025 Oral, arXiv:2503.07067) is the current SOTA white-box LLM-distillation objective. Its core insight: prior KD applies the SAME loss to teacher-generated and student-generated data, which is suboptimal because the two data types want opposite skew. DistiLLM-2 uses a CONTRASTIVE asymmetric loss that simultaneously raises the likelihood of teacher responses and lowers the likelihood of student responses.

MATH (verified against the reference impl src/distillm_trainer.py):
  Let p = teacher next-token dist, q_theta = student. Skewed-KL with skew alpha:
    SKL^alpha(p||q) = KL( p || alpha*p + (1-alpha)*q )   -- the mixture lives in the SECOND slot so log-ratios never blow up; gives stable gradients (DistiLLM v1 result, arXiv:2402.03898).
    SRKL^alpha(p||q) = KL( q || (1-alpha)*p + alpha*q )   -- reverse direction, student in numerator.
  In the repo these are computed in log-space per token. For the TEACHER (chosen) responses:
    mix = logsumexp( log(alpha)+tea_logps , log(1-alpha)+stu_logps )  ; tea_pos_kl = sum( exp(tea)*(tea - mix) )   == SKL.
  For the STUDENT (rejected) responses (student side gets weight alpha, student logps DETACHED on the teacher-anchor term):
    mix = logsumexp( log(1-alpha)+tea_logps , log(alpha)+stu_logps_detached ) ; ref_pos_kl = sum( exp(stu)*(stu - mix) ) == SRKL.
  Final per-batch loss (loss_type=='distillm_v2'):
    L = (2 - beta)*chosen_position_kl + beta*rejected_position_kl
    where chosen_position_kl=SKL on teacher-generated y_t, rejected_position_kl=SRKL on student-generated y_s.

CURRICULUM (two schedules, both in the reference impl):
  (1) Adaptive per-sample alpha (CALD / curriculum): alpha_t = clip( 1 - (1-base_alpha)*anchor / (sentence_logp_gap + eps), 1e-2, base_alpha ). Hard samples (large teacher-student gap) get larger skew, easy samples smaller. base_alpha default 0.1.
  (2) Gradual beta ramp: beta = 1.0 + 0.5*min(1, 2*global_step/max_steps). Starts emphasizing the teacher term (SKL on chosen), ramps the student-correction term (SRKL on rejected) as the student improves.

DATA FLOW (off-policy + on-policy contrastive pairs): DistiLLM-2 reuses the trl DPO data shape: each row = {prompt, chosen=teacher_generated_response, rejected=student_generated_response}. The student responses are produced ON-POLICY by sampling from the current student (vLLM, temperature 0.8) once before/per stage, then reformatted into chosen/rejected pairs (generate/generate_vllm.py + generate/reformat.py). The trainer needs BOTH a trainable student AND a frozen teacher resident to compute teacher logits over both response branches (the teacher is the DPO 'ref_model').

PIPELINE PLACEMENT in kolm: this is the DISTILL stage objective, slotting where workers/distill/distill.mjs --mode=full hands off to a python trainer, and is the substantive upgrade to the apps/trainer/distill.py logit-KD engine. It sits AFTER CURATE/AUGMENT produce the prompt set + teacher responses, and AFTER (or interleaved with) an on-policy student-generation step. Its output (LoRA adapter + run-meta loss receipt) feeds COMPILE -> K-score gate -> .kolm artifact.
specs.kolm_current: kolm has TWO trainer stacks (per audit/w921/studio.discovery.json). (1) The SHIPPING path the W920 audit covered: workers/distill/distill.mjs -> workers/distill/scripts/train_lora.py does SeqKD ONLY -- DataCollatorForLanguageModeling(mlm=False), labels=input_ids.copy(), vanilla LoraConfig; no teacher logits consumed (distill.mjs:319-321,443 only ever invoke train_lora.py). (2) A richer apps/trainer/ stack that the shipping path does NOT use. apps/trainer/distill.py:536-646 implements token-level FORWARD_KL (KL(student||teacher), Hinton T^2), REVERSE_KL (KL(teacher||student), MiniLLM), and JSD via _KD_FNS; compute_loss (distill.py:1075-1134) runs the teacher as a LIVE local model (teacher(input_ids=...) every batch under no_grad) and forms L = alpha*loss_kd + (1-alpha)*loss_ce, alpha=0.9 T=2.0. It has a DistillConfig.on_policy flag (distill.py:579-583) but no on-policy generation loop is wired. apps/trainer/contrastive_distill.py:110-130 implements a W714-2 RESPONSE-LEVEL contrastive_loss L = L_kd(s,pos) - lam*mean(L_kd(s,neg_i)) plus a token_level_dpo_loss (W827), reachable via `kolm distill --contrastive` (cli/kolm.js:20801 -> cmdDistillContrastive). NONE of these is the DistiLLM-2 asymmetric SKL/SRKL formulation: there is no skewed mixture (logsumexp(log(alpha)+tea, log(1-alpha)+stu)), no SRKL, no (2-beta)*SKL+beta*SRKL combination, no adaptive-alpha or gradual-beta curriculum, and no teacher-generated-vs-student-generated chosen/rejected curriculum. The recipe loader (src/distill-recipe-loader.js:68 VALID_TRAIN_METHODS=['qlora','lora','full']) has NO objective/loss field at all -- a recipe cannot even request a logit-KD loss today. Teacher capture (workers/distill/teacher-bridge.mjs:107-196) returns TEXT ONLY (response string); it captures no logprobs/logits/top_logprobs (grep confirmed zero hits), so for API teachers (anthropic/openai/google/xai) no teacher distribution exists -- DistiLLM-2 is only feasible when the teacher is a LOCAL HF model (the proven W869 DeepSeek-R1-32B-INT4-on-5090 served path).
specs.gap: kolm markets 'distillation' but the DEFAULT shipping path trains plain SeqKD on teacher text strings, and even the richer apps/trainer engine tops out at vanilla forward/reverse KL + a response-level contrastive term. The single SOTA distillation objective -- DistiLLM-2's asymmetric SKL-on-teacher-data + SRKL-on-student-data with curriculum -- which MEMORY says W869 explicitly cited for the Trinity recipe, is NOT implemented anywhere. Concretely missing: (a) skewed-KL (SKL) with a stable log-space mixture; (b) skewed-reverse-KL (SRKL); (c) the contrastive (2-beta)*SKL+beta*SRKL combination keyed on teacher-generated vs student-generated branches; (d) on-policy student response generation to produce the 'rejected' branch; (e) adaptive per-sample alpha and gradual-beta curriculum; (f) a recipe-loader objective/loss section so a recipe can select it; (g) for the LOCAL-teacher case, wiring the existing live-teacher forward pass (apps/trainer/distill.py) into the new loss. This is the highest-substance differentiator vs distil labs / OpenPipe / Lamini, which are essentially SFT platforms, and it is what makes the K-score T-axis 'beats Claude/GPT-4o-mini' claims defensible as real distillation rather than imitation SFT.
specs.improvement: Implement DistiLLM-2 as a first-class distillation OBJECTIVE inside the apps/trainer engine and expose it through the shipping recipe loader, gated to the LOCAL-teacher regime (where teacher logits exist) with an honest SeqKD fallback for API teachers. Build it as an additive KDObjective + on-policy generation + curriculum, NOT a new divergent trainer. Steps: (1) Add KDObjective.DISTILLM2 and DistillConfig fields (base_alpha_1, base_alpha_2, gradual_beta, adaptive_alpha, on_policy_gen) to apps/trainer/distill.py. (2) Add pure functions skewed_kl(), skewed_reverse_kl(), distillm2_loss() and adaptive_alpha()/gradual_beta() ported from the reference log-space implementation, with unit tests at fixed logits. (3) Extend compute_loss to: when objective==DISTILLM2, compute SKL on the teacher-branch (chosen) tokens and SRKL on the student-branch (rejected) tokens and combine via (2-beta)*SKL+beta*SRKL; the existing live-teacher forward pass supplies teacher logits for both branches. (4) Add an on-policy generation pre-pass (generate_student_responses()) that samples y_s from the current student (HF .generate, temperature 0.8, batched) and writes chosen/rejected rows -- reuse the existing teacher_output as 'chosen'. (5) Add a recipe-loader 'distill' section {objective, loss, base_alpha, gradual_beta, on_policy} (src/distill-recipe-loader.js) with fail-before-spend validation. specs.risk: MEDIUM-HIGH. (1) Hard constraint: DistiLLM-2 needs teacher LOGITS, but kolm's primary teachers are API (text-only) -- so the feature only applies to the local-teacher regime (the proven DeepSeek-R1-32B-INT4-on-5090 path). Mitigation: gate strictly + honest SeqKD fallback; this is a real scope limit, not a bug. (2) VRAM: keeping a frozen teacher AND a trainable student resident on one 5090 is tight (W869 note: bf16 7B-teacher + student already near the edge). Mitigation: INT4 teacher (bnb NF4, already proven), gradient checkpointing, small batch+grad-accum, top_k logit pruning (already in _topk_prune). (3) On-policy generation cost: sampling student responses each stage doubles forward work; mitigate with periodic (per-epoch) regen, not per-step, matching the reference impl. (4) Numerical stability of the log-space mixture (handled by logsumexp + clip in the reference). (5) Two-trainer divergence: building this only in apps/trainer while the SHIPPING default remains workers/distill/train_lora.py means the marketed objective is opt-in -- a follow-on unification wave (discovery candidate #2) is needed to make it the default. (6) Should NOT be confused with the existing W714-2 response-level contrastive_loss in contrastive_distill.py, which is a different (DPO-flavored) objective -- keep them distinct and labeled in the receipt.

demo.target_experience: A first-time visitor lands on /demo-live and watches what is unmistakably a recording of a real terminal session: the kolm loop run end-to-end on real hardware, paced with human-imperfect timing. Four beats, one idea each - 1. Capture, 2. Compile (distill->quantize INT4), 3. Verify, 4. Run. Every visible value is real and sourced: the model is Qwen2.5 distilled from the real Claude 3.5 Sonnet + GPT-4o + DeepSeek-R1-32B council; the INT4 numbers are the X04-blocking measurements (61.0 GB -> 17.9 GB in 125.3s, 11.5 tok/s on an RTX 5090, named as such, no M3 Max sleight of hand); the captures are realistically messy support tickets with a real shape, PII-redacted on screen. The climax is proof-as-performance: the demo prints a REAL receipt CID and the /verify URL, then the viewer clicks 'Verify this receipt' and watches the page recompute the canonical hash and check the Ed25519 signature IN THE BROWSER against the public key embedded in the receipt - no login, no network, no trust required. The whole thing is keyboard-operable, screen-reader-narrated via the caption track, falls back to a static poster under prefers-reduced-motion, and presents as one focused pane per beat on mobile. demo.data_strategy:
Replace the invented CHAPTERS data source with a CAPTURED, checked-in timeline produced by an actual kolm run, and make the climax verifiable client-side with zero network dependence. Concretely:

(1) GROUND TRUTH FILES that already exist and are X04-blocking: public/benchmarks/sota-quantize-matrix.json (DeepSeek-R1-32B 61.03 GB BF16 -> 17.9 GB INT4 in 125.26s, 11.53 tok/s, 19.22 GB VRAM, 13.3s load on RTX 5090, CUDA 12.8, torch 2.11.0+cu128, bitsandbytes 0.49.2 NF4+double; Qwen2.5-7B 14.2->5.18 GB in 29.2s @ 24.5 tok/s; Qwen2.5-3B 5.76->1.92 GB in 13.85s; Qwen2.5-0.5B 0.93->0.44 GB in 11.59s) and public/benchmarks/trinity-500-benchmark.json (council = Claude 3.5 Sonnet + GPT-4o + DeepSeek-R1-32B distilled to Qwen2.5-7B via LoRA r=16 alpha=32, 410 pairs, 79.18s, n=57 holdout: 96.5% asks-1Q, 100% no-inventions, 100% judge-clarify, 100% judge-on-policy, 1.24s mean latency, 210 mean chars - beats base-qwen, claude-haiku-4-5, ties gpt-4o-mini at fewer chars) and public/benchmarks/wave887-wrapper-prod-benchmark.json (gateway mean 2478ms, +703ms / +40% overhead vs direct, $0.307 vs $0.301 per 1k, 10/10 receipts, example receipt rcpt_01KYC1ZV98HBEHW0NFC5DB signed by key c2e942e24061e9314790d73ce7b3f351). EVERY headline number in the new demo MUST be read from one of these files (or its on-disk source) and MUST match an x04-claim-fixtures.json row. No literal that isn't sourced.

(2) CAPTURE PIPELINE (new, under scripts/, the only product-code file this spec authorizes touching besides the HTML): scripts/capture-demo-timeline.mjs runs the REAL CLI (cli/kolm.js) against committed fixtures and serializes a timeline. Steps it captures: `kolm capture` / gateway proxy over a fixtures file of realistically-messy support tickets (data/demo/support-tickets.jsonl - committed, PII-redacted shape) -> real capture rows with real cap_ ids and real per-call token/cost; `kolm distill` / `kolm compile` of that namespace against the council teachers -> real step/loss lines and a real compile receipt; `kolm quantize ... int4` -> the real sota-quantize-matrix figures for the chosen model; `kolm serve` + one real `/v1/chat/completions` reply -> real tok/s + latency for that model on the capturing machine; `kolm receipts verify --offline <receipt>` -> the real Ed25519 verification transcript. The script writes public/demo-live-timeline.json: an array of beats, each with {label, events:[{at, type, text|html, klass}]} in the SAME event vocabulary the existing engine already understands (type/out/page/progress/receipt/capture/prompt/enter/subtitle/end), PLUS the real artifacts (a full signed receipt blob, the captures, the metrics). The HTML's CHAPTERS array is deleted and replaced by `fetch('/demo-live-timeline.json')` at boot (with the last-captured timeline inlined as a fallback so the page never blanks).

(3) THE VERIFIABLE CLIMAX (no network, no trust): commit the FULL real signed receipt JSON (a complete kolm-audit-1 receipt with its signature_ed25519 block, captured from the run) into public/demo-live-timeline.json. In the Verify beat, the page does what src/gateway-receipt.js verifyReceipt does, but in the BROWSER: rebuild the canonical payload = JSON.stringify over receipt-schema.js ALL_FIELDS order with signature_ed25519 stripped (the exact 20-field order: schema, receipt_id, timestamp, namespace_id, route_decision, provider, model, artifact_id, confidence, fallback_reason, input_hash, output_hash, capture_eligible, capture_id, redaction_applied, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd, signing_key_id, verify_url), then call crypto.subtle.verify('Ed25519', publicKey, signature, canonicalBytes) using the public_key embedded in the receipt's signature_ed25519 block. Because the public key travels with the receipt, this verifies with no secret and no server. The viewer clicks 'Verify this receipt' and watches the canonical hash recompute and the signature check go green in front of them - proof-as-performance. The receipt's verify_url (https://kolm.ai/v1/verify/<id>) is shown and clickable as a PROGRESSIVE ENHANCEMENT: if prod still has the row it resolves; if prod's DB was wiped (the known Railway constraint - signing_key currently 'missing' and both candidate receipts return receipt_not_found today) the in-browser check still passes, so the climax can NEVER 404. This is the single most important design decision in the spec: the proof is self-contained in the committed receipt, not dependent on live prod state.

(4) THE REAL RECEIPT ID: use the receipt that the capture pipeline actually produces and commits. Do NOT reuse rcpt_compile_71F2A6 (invented) or rcpt_01KYC1ZVTGDCW3FX06JQSC (a verify.html placeholder that does not resolve). If a fresh capture is not run before ship, fall back to rcpt_01KYC1ZV98HBEHW0NFC5DB which is the X04-blocking example_receipt_id in wave887-wrapper-prod-benchmark.json (signing key c2e942e24061e9314790d73ce7b3f351) - but ONLY if its full signed blob is committed so in-browser verification works; the bare ID alone is not enough.

(5) NO NUMBER WITHOUT A SOURCE: add a release-verify gate (extend scripts/x04-claim-verify.cjs coverage or a sibling scripts/demo-claim-verify.cjs) that walks public/demo-live-timeline.json and asserts every numeric/metric literal it emits matches an x04-claim-fixtures.json row or a benchmarks/*.json field. The demo can then never out-claim a measurement. Reconcile or remove the fabricated arithmetic (DEMO-7) by deriving spend/cost from the captured token counts, not by typing a round number.
build_steps: STEP 0 - Decide the headline beat. Lead Compile with the strongest X04-verified number: the INT4 quantize stopwatch landing on '61.0 GB -> 17.9 GB in 125.3s, 11.5 tok/s on RTX 5090' (sota-quantize-matrix 32B row). Name the hardware explicitly (RTX 5090, CUDA 12.8) - kill the M3 Max claim entirely (DEMO-4). The student/distill story uses the real council (Claude 3.5 Sonnet + GPT-4o + DeepSeek-R1-32B -> Qwen2.5-7B) from trinity-500-benchmark.json (DEMO-2 fix).

STEP 1 - Author committed fixtures: data/demo/support-tickets.jsonl with ~12-19 realistically messy, PII-redacted support tickets (real shape: typos, order IDs, multi-intent, refunds, password resets) so the capture beat shows a non-trivial real input, not a clean toy (PATTERN 9).

STEP 2 - Build scripts/capture-demo-timeline.mjs: run the real CLI (kolm capture/gateway -> distill/compile -> quantize int4 -> serve + one real reply -> receipts verify --offline) against the fixtures on a real machine, and serialize the run to public/demo-live-timeline.json using the existing event vocabulary (type/out/page/progress/receipt/capture/prompt/enter/subtitle/end). Capture real cap_ ids, real token/cost per call, real step/loss lines from distill, the real quantize figures, the real serve tok/s+latency for the served model, and the FULL signed receipt blob (with signature_ed25519). Use VHS-style Hide/Show trimming of setup keystrokes but never retouch output frames (PATTERN 6).

STEP 3 - Rip out the invented data source in demo-live.html: delete the hardcoded CHAPTERS array (288-439), PAGES literals that assert fake routes (510-521), and the three receipt builders (442-507). Replace with `const TIMELINE = await fetch('/demo-live-timeline.json').then(r=>r.json()).catch(()=>INLINE_FALLBACK)` where INLINE_FALLBACK is the last committed capture inlined so the page never blanks offline. Keep the engine (tick loop, typeText, outLine, setPage, setReceipt, addCapture, seek, scrub) intact - it is the part the user wants reused.

STEP 4 - Collapse 6 chapters to 4 substantive beats (PATTERN 7): 1. Capture, 2. Compile (distill + INT4 quantize, the money shot with the stopwatch), 3. Verify (the climax), 4. Run (serve + 'try your own ticket'). Fold the old Setup chapter into a single preamble caption; fold Try-it into the end-card's ONE primary CTA.

STEP 5 - Implement the in-browser Ed25519 verify (the climax): add verifyReceiptInBrowser(receipt) that (a) rebuilds canonical = JSON.stringify over ALL_FIELDS order minus signature_ed25519, (b) imports the embedded public_key as a CryptoKey, (c) crypto.subtle.verify('Ed25519', key, sig, new TextEncoder().encode(canonical)). Wire a real 'Verify this receipt' button in the Verify beat / receipt pane that runs it on click and animates the canonical-hash recompute + signature check to green. Show the verify_url as a clickable link (progressive enhancement; never the source of truth).

STEP 6 - Source every number: thread the real values from the captured timeline + benchmarks/*.json into the terminal lines, receipt pane, browser pages, and end-card stats. Derive spend from captured token counts (kill DEMO-7 double-count). Fix the captures count so the dashboard total, the rendered rows, and the compile 'approving N' all agree (single source = captured rows length).

STEP 7 - Fix the install line (DEMO-3): replace 'curl -fsSL kolm.ai/get | sh' with the real install ('npm i -g @kolm/cli' or the pip-from-source command per W490) in both the Try-it command and PAGES.home. Confirm the command resolves before shipping.

STEP 8 - Replace fake browser-bar routes with real ones or mark them clearly as local (DEMO-6/links): the gateway/serve URLs are genuinely localhost (127.0.0.1:8766) so label them as local; the account/verify URLs must be real routes or removed.

STEP 9 - Engine hardening (DEMO-8): make typeText resumable after pause (persist index in closure-free state so resume continues from where it stopped); add small per-character timing variance + a believable pre-result beat so typing is not uniform; remove the dead 'wait'/'browserPatch' comment claims or implement them; keep CursorBlink on (gated by reduced-motion).

STEP 10 - Reduced-motion + poster (A11Y-4, PATTERN 10): generate public/demo-live-poster.png (a still of the final verified-receipt frame) via the recorder; when prefers-reduced-motion is set OR ?still=1, render the poster + the full transcript as static selectable text and a working 'Verify this receipt' button instead of auto-playing the 70s cascade.

STEP 11 - A11y pass: make chapter chips real <button> with tabindex, keydown (Enter/Space), and aria-current on the active beat (A11Y-3); update scrub aria-valuenow + add aria-valuetext ('00:24, Compile') on every tick/seek and add :focus-visible (A11Y-1); move focus into the end-card on show, trap it, Esc-dismiss, mark the stage inert/aria-hidden while open, and aria-labelledby the heading (A11Y-2); put the subtitle caption in an aria-live=polite region and DROP aria-live from the noisy terminal (A11Y-5); aria-hidden the decorative lights/dots (A11Y-6).

STEP 12 - Mobile redesign (MOBILE-1/2/3): under 1000px show ONE focused pane per beat (terminal for Capture/Compile, receipt for Verify, browser for Run) with a beat switcher, instead of three crushed 1fr rows; make the chapter strip keyboard/tab reachable with a visible scroll affordance; ensure the end-card is one clean screen on short viewports.

STEP 13 - Fix the recorder (DEMO-9/10): add ?record=1 to demo-live.html that DISABLES the 500ms auto-play so the recorder is the sole play trigger; in record-demo-w905.mjs check play-state before clicking (or rely on ?record=1), default URL to a pinned LOCAL build not live prod, and set RECORD_MS from the timeline's actual TOTAL so there is no dead tail.

STEP 14 - Add the release-verify gate: scripts/demo-claim-verify.cjs (or extend x04-claim-verify.cjs) walks public/demo-live-timeline.json, asserts every emitted number matches an x04-claim-fixtures.json row / benchmarks field, and asserts the committed receipt blob verifies via the same canonical+Ed25519 path the browser uses. Wire it into scripts/release-verify.cjs so a drifting or out-claiming demo blocks release.

STEP 15 - Add a 'Run this yourself' strip + per-command Copy buttons (PATTERN 2/8): below the player, list the exact copy-pasteable commands from the timeline; in the Run beat, expose a 'paste your own ticket' input wired to the real public /v1/free/chat path (POST {question}) so the viewer can break it with their own scenario (PATTERN 12). Note free-chat expects {question}, not OpenAI {messages}.
STEP 16 - Update meta/SEO/JSON-LD to match the new real numbers and the 4-beat / ~60-70s structure; bump public/sw.js cache version per the project's deploy ritual.
polish_checklist: Motion: typing has small per-character variance and a believable beat before each result lands (no uniform 26 cps); cursor blinks during typing, freezes on enter; progress bars ease; page/receipt swaps cross-fade <= 260ms. No animation performs for its own sake (Linear restraint).

Reduced-motion: prefers-reduced-motion (and ?still=1) serves a static public/demo-live-poster.png of the final verified-receipt frame + full selectable transcript + a working 'Verify this receipt' button; no auto-playing cascade, no blink, no fade.
A11y - chips: chapter chips are real buttons, tabbable, Enter/Space operable, aria-current on active beat.
A11y - scrub: aria-valuenow + aria-valuetext updated on every tick and seek; visible :focus-visible ring; ArrowLeft/Right still seek.
A11y - dialog: end-card moves focus in on show, traps focus, Esc dismisses, stage is inert/aria-hidden while open, dialog aria-labelledby its heading.
A11y - narration: subtitle caption is an aria-live=polite region; the terminal is NOT a live region (no flood); decorative lights/dots are aria-hidden.
A11y - autoplay: respects WCAG 2.2.2 (pause/stop control present) AND does not auto-start motion for reduced-motion users.

Mobile: one focused pane per beat under 1000px with a keyboard-reachable beat switcher; chapter strip has a visible scroll affordance; end-card is one clean screen on short/landscape viewports; tap targets >= 44px.

No dead links: install command resolves (npm/pip, not /get); verify_url is shown but the climax does not depend on it; in-browser verify passes offline; any displayed route either resolves or is clearly labeled local.

Authenticity: every visible number traces to x04-claim-fixtures.json / benchmarks/*.json; the receipt blob is real and re-verifiable in-browser; the model/teacher names are real (Qwen2.5; Claude 3.5 Sonnet + GPT-4o + DeepSeek-R1-32B council); hardware is named correctly (RTX 5090).
Copy discipline: surfaced copy uses 'verifiable' / 'reproducible' / 'proof you can check' / 'constraints' - never the word this project's MEMORY bans.

Review pass: full keyboard-only walkthrough; VoiceOver/NVDA narration of the 4 beats via caption track; demo-claim-verify gate green in release-verify; recorder produces a clean MP4 from a pinned local build with no dead tail; side-by-side of the captured terminal vs a real CLI run to confirm parity.
acceptance_criteria: No invented receipt ID anywhere: rcpt_compile_71F2A6 is gone; the Verify climax uses a receipt whose FULL signed blob is committed and re-verifiable in-browser.

Clicking 'Verify this receipt' recomputes the canonical payload (ALL_FIELDS order, signature_ed25519 stripped) and verifies the Ed25519 signature via crypto.subtle against the embedded public key, going green with ZERO network calls - and it still passes when prod /v1/verify returns receipt_not_found.
claude-3.7-haiku appears zero times; the teacher story names the real council (Claude 3.5 Sonnet + GPT-4o + DeepSeek-R1-32B) and the student is a real Qwen2.5 model.

11.5 tok/s and all INT4 figures are attributed to the correct model+hardware (DeepSeek-R1-32B INT4 on RTX 5090) and match sota-quantize-matrix.json / their x04-claim-fixtures rows exactly; no M3 Max attribution remains.

Every numeric/metric literal emitted by the demo matches an x04-claim-fixtures.json row or a benchmarks/*.json field; scripts/demo-claim-verify.cjs passes and is wired into release-verify so drift blocks release.
The install command shown resolves (npm i -g @kolm/cli or pip-from-source); 'curl ... kolm.ai/get | sh' is gone.
Capture count is internally consistent: dashboard total == rendered rows source == compile 'approving N'; spend is derived from captured token counts, not a typed round number (DEMO-7 resolved).

The timeline is sourced from public/demo-live-timeline.json produced by scripts/capture-demo-timeline.mjs running the real CLI; the HTML no longer hardcodes a CHAPTERS array; an inline fallback prevents a blank page offline.
prefers-reduced-motion (and ?still=1) serves a static poster + full transcript + working verify button; no auto-playing cascade for those users.

Full keyboard operability: chapter chips are buttons with aria-current; scrub updates aria-valuenow/valuetext with visible focus; end-card is a focus-trapped, Esc-dismissable dialog with focus moved in and stage inert.
Subtitle caption is the screen-reader narration (aria-live); the terminal no longer floods the SR.
Mobile shows one focused pane per beat with a keyboard-reachable switcher; no three-crushed-rows layout; end-card is one clean screen.
Recorder: ?record=1 disables auto-play, recorder is the sole play trigger, targets a pinned local build, and RECORD_MS derives from the timeline TOTAL (no dead tail).
The exact commands in the demo are copy-pasteable (per-command Copy) and the same as the quickstart; the Run beat's 'paste your own ticket' hits the real public /v1/free/chat ({question}) path.
sw.js cache version bumped; meta/JSON-LD reflect the new real numbers and 4-beat structure; the page reads as finished and reviewed.
